data.text: I'll start by reading the key files mentioned in the bead before implementing anything.

input.description: Explore codebase structure for bead implementation
input.prompt:
I need to understand the current state of these files in the DDx codebase at /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-73c07039-20260511T014529-858e9a1c:

1. Read cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go - full file
2. Read cli/internal/bead/store.go - full file
3. Find any files related to "metrics", "analyze", "work analyze" commands in cli/cmd/
4. Find any ac-check related files in cli/internal/
5. List all files in cli/internal/bead/ directory
6. List all files in cli/internal/agent/ directory
7. List all files in cli/cmd/ directory
8. Check if there's any existing event types for review-accuracy-override or review-ac-override

Please read all these files and report the full content of execute_bead_review.go and store.go, and the directory listings for the other items.

23	// beadStrictnessMode returns the strictness mode for review based on bead
24	// labels. kind:doc and kind:mechanical → mechanical; kind:refactor and
25	// kind:chore → behavior-light; default → strict.
26	func beadStrictnessMode(labels []string) string {
27		for _, l := range labels {
28			switch l {
29			case "kind:doc", "kind:mechanical":
30				return StrictnessMechanical
31			case "kind:refactor", "kind:chore":
32				return StrictnessBehavior
33			}
34		}
35		return StrictnessStrict
36	}
37	
38	// strictnessModeInstructions returns the per-mode evidence requirement text
39	// injected into the reviewer prompt.
40	func strictnessModeInstructions(mode string) string {
41		switch mode {
42		case StrictnessMechanical:
43			return "mechanical — file presence, renames, or symbol evidence only; no test-name or runtime evidence required."
44		case StrictnessBehavior:
45			return "behavior-light — build green plus file/symbol evidence suffices; test-name match required only when an AC explicitly names a Test* function."
46		default:
47			return "strict — each AC must be anchored to a named Test* function or a diff-touched symbol; file-only evidence is insufficient."
48		}
49	}
50	
51	// ReviewRequestClarificationEventKind is the event kind appended to a bead
52	// when the reviewer returns REQUEST_CLARIFICATION.
53	const ReviewRequestClarificationEventKind = "review-request-clarification"
54	
55	// ReviewPairingDegradedEventKind is the kind:review-pairing-degraded event
56	// kind appended to the bead when the post-merge reviewer was routed to the
57	// same provider as the implementer. R4 (different reviewer) is best-effort
58	// emergent: bumping MinPower usually picks a different candidate, but the
59	// guarantee is not absolute. The event surfaces the degradation so operators
60	// can see when reviewer diversity collapsed.
61	const ReviewPairingDegradedEventKind = "review-pairing-degraded"
62	
63	// ReviewerEscalatedEventKind is the kind:reviewer-escalated event kind
64	// appended when the reviewer's MinPower is bumped above the baseline
65	// impl.ActualPower+1 due to prior review-error or review-pairing-degraded
66	// events for the same result_rev.
67	const ReviewerEscalatedEventKind = "reviewer-escalated"
68	
69	// ImplementerRouting captures the implementer's resolved routing details so
70	// the post-merge reviewer can build a paired ExecuteRequest with Role=reviewer,
71	// the same correlation, and MinPower bumped one above the implementer's
72	// actual selected power (R4 pairing).
73	type ImplementerRouting struct {
74		Harness     string
75		Provider    string
76		Model       string
77		ActualPower int
78		// Correlation is the implementer's correlation map. The reviewer copies
79		// its keys into its own dispatch metadata so events / session log /
80		// aggregations can join the two calls.
81		Correlation map[string]string
82	}
83	
84	// Verdict is the outcome of a post-merge bead review.
85	type Verdict string
86	
87	const (
88		// VerdictApprove means all AC items passed; the bead stays closed.
89		VerdictApprove Verdict = "APPROVE"
90		// VerdictRequestChanges means some AC items need fixing; the bead stays open
91		// and the pre-close gate records the verdict without closing.
92		VerdictRequestChanges Verdict = "REQUEST_CHANGES"
93		// VerdictBlock means escalation should stop; the bead is flagged for human
94		// review while remaining open.
95		VerdictBlock Verdict = "BLOCK"
96		// VerdictRequestClarification means the reviewer cannot adjudicate one or
97		// more needs-judgment AC items without additional operator context. Unlike
98		// BLOCK it does not halt automated retry; it parks the bead to
99		// status=proposed so the operator can supply the missing context.
100		VerdictRequestClarification Verdict = "REQUEST_CLARIFICATION"
101	)
102	
103	// Strictness mode constants drive the per-bead evidence requirements injected
104	// into the reviewer prompt. The mode is derived from bead labels.
105	const (
106		// StrictnessStrict requires named Test* functions and diff-anchored symbols
107		// per AC. Applied to kind:fix and kind:feat beads.
108		StrictnessStrict = "strict"
109		// StrictnessBehavior requires build-green plus file/symbol evidence.
110		// Test-name match is required only when an AC explicitly names a Test*.
111		// Applied to kind:refactor and kind:chore beads.
112		StrictnessBehavior = "behavior-light"
113		// StrictnessMechanical requires only file presence, renames, or symbol
114		// evidence. Applied to kind:doc and kind:mechanical beads.
115		StrictnessMechanical = "mechanical"
116	)

213	// SelectReviewerTier returns the tier to use for the review agent.
214	// Rule: max(impl_tier + 1, smart). Since smart is the ceiling, the
215	// reviewer always runs at smart tier regardless of the implementation tier.
216	func SelectReviewerTier(_ escalation.ModelTier) escalation.ModelTier {
217		return escalation.TierSmart
218	}

220	// HasBeadLabel reports whether label is present in labels.
221	func HasBeadLabel(labels []string, label string) bool {
222		for _, l := range labels {
223			if l == label {
224				return true
225			}
226		}
227		return false
228	}

230	// HasBeadLabelPrefix reports whether any label starts with the provided prefix.

235			}
236		}

240	// BeadReader can fetch a bead by ID. Implemented by *bead.Store.
241	type BeadReader interface {
242		Get(id string) (*bead.Bead, error)
243	}
244	
245	// BeadEventReader reads the event history recorded against a bead.
246	// Implemented by *bead.Store. Used by DefaultBeadReviewer to count prior
247	// review escalation triggers for the same result_rev.
248	type BeadEventReader interface {

250	}

252	// BeadReviewer runs a post-merge review for a completed bead.
253	type BeadReviewer interface {
254		ReviewBead(ctx context.Context, beadID, resultRev string, impl ImplementerRouting) (*ReviewResult, error)
255	}

257	// reviewGroupReviewer is the optional pre-close gate used by execute-loop.
258	// DefaultBeadReviewer implements it; test doubles can opt in when they need
259	// to exercise the unanimous-two-slot path explicitly.
260	type reviewGroupReviewer interface {
261		ReviewGroup(ctx context.Context, beadID, resultRev string, impl ImplementerRouting) (*ReviewGroupResult, error)
262	}
263	
264	// beadReviewInstructions is the review contract embedded in the prompt.
265	// The reviewing agent must produce a single JSON object matching the
266	// ReviewVerdict schema (schema_version: 1). The markdown contract this
267	// replaces silently mis-parsed `### Verdict: APPROVE` outputs whenever the
268	// model echoed the prompt's options header — see review_verdict.go.
269	const beadReviewInstructions = `You are reviewing a bead implementation against its acceptance criteria.
270	
271	## AC-Check Ratification
272	
273	When an <ac-check> section is present, ratify the mechanical results rather
274	than re-verifying them independently from the diff:
275	
276	- result="pass": confirm the evidence is credible. Override to fail only if
277	  the evidence is fabricated — include judgment_override_reason and a diff
278	  citation (file:line) in the per_ac evidence string.
279	- result="fail": mechanically verified failure. Grade as fail and BLOCK unless
280	  the commit message contains an explicit AC-Waive trailer for this AC.
281	- result="needs_judgment": adjudicate from the diff. If you cannot determine
282	  pass/fail without additional bead context from the operator, use
283	  REQUEST_CLARIFICATION for that AC item.
284	- result="error": treat as needs_judgment.
285	
286	Overriding a mechanical grade (pass→fail or fail→pass) requires an explicit
287	judgment_override_reason note and a concrete diff citation in the evidence.
288	
289	## Strictness Mode
290	
291	The <strictness-mode> tag specifies per-bead evidence requirements:
292	
293	- strict (kind:fix, kind:feat): each AC must be anchored to a named Test*
294	  function or a diff-touched symbol; file-only evidence is insufficient.
295	- behavior-light (kind:refactor, kind:chore): build green plus file/symbol
296	  evidence suffices; test-name match required only when an AC explicitly
297	  names a Test* function.
298	- mechanical (kind:doc, kind:mechanical): file presence, renames, or symbol
299	  evidence only; no test-name or runtime evidence required.
300	
301	## Verdicts
302	
303	For each acceptance-criteria (AC) item, decide whether it is implemented
304	correctly, then assign one overall verdict:
305	
306	- APPROVE — every AC item is fully and correctly implemented.
307	- REQUEST_CHANGES — some AC items are partial or have fixable minor issues.
308	- BLOCK — at least one AC item is not implemented or incorrectly implemented;
309	  or the diff is insufficient to evaluate.
310	- REQUEST_CLARIFICATION — you cannot adjudicate one or more needs_judgment AC
311	  items without operator clarification. Use this ONLY when the item is
312	  ambiguous even given the full diff. This verdict does NOT block the queue;
313	  it routes to the operator lane for input.
314	
315	## Required output format (schema_version: 1)
316	
317	Respond with EXACTLY one JSON object as your final response, fenced as a single ` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + `json … ` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + ` code block. Do not include any prose outside the fenced block. The JSON must match this schema:
318	
319	` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + `json
320	{
321	  "schema_version": 1,
322	  "verdict": "APPROVE",
323	  "summary": "≤300 char human-readable verdict justification",
324	  "per_ac": [
325	    { "number": 1, "item": "acceptance criterion text", "grade": "pass", "evidence": "file:line or test evidence" }
326	  ],
327	  "findings": [
328	    { "severity": "info", "summary": "what is wrong or notable", "location": "path/to/file.go:42" }
329	  ]
330	}
331	` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + `
332	
333	Rules:
334	- "verdict" must be exactly one of "APPROVE", "REQUEST_CHANGES", "BLOCK", "REQUEST_CLARIFICATION".
335	- "severity" must be exactly one of "info", "warn", "block".
336	- Output the JSON object inside ONE fenced ` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + `json … ` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + ` block. No additional prose, no extra fences, no markdown headings.
337	- Do not echo this template back. Do not write the verdict value anywhere except as the JSON value of the verdict field.`
338	
339	// BuildReviewPromptOptions configures evidence-bounded prompt assembly.
340	// FEAT-022 §5/§7: caps drive per-section trimming and the pre-dispatch
341	// short-circuit on residual oversize.
342	type BuildReviewPromptOptions struct {
343		Caps evidence.Caps
344		// ACCheckJSON, when non-empty, is the raw JSON content of the
345		// .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ac-check.json file produced by
346		// `ddx bead ac-check`. Injected as a structured <ac-check> section so
347		// the reviewer ratifies mechanical results rather than re-grepping the diff.
348		ACCheckJSON string
349	}
350	
351	// ReviewPromptOptions controls optional prompt sections.
352	type ReviewPromptOptions struct {
353		IncludeProse bool
354	}
355	
356	// BuildReviewPromptResult is the structured output of BuildReviewPromptBounded.
357	// Overflow is true when, after all per-section trimming, the assembled
358	// prompt still exceeds Caps.MaxPromptBytes; callers MUST NOT dispatch in
359	// that case (FEAT-022 §7).
360	type BuildReviewPromptResult struct {
361		Prompt   string
362		Overflow bool
363		Sections []evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection
364	}
365	
366	// BuildReviewPrompt builds the complete review prompt for a bead implementation
367	// using default caps. Callers needing overflow detection use
368	// BuildReviewPromptBounded.
369	func BuildReviewPrompt(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef) string {
370		return BuildReviewPromptBounded(b, iter, rev, diff, projectRoot, refs, BuildReviewPromptOptions{Caps: evidence.DefaultCaps()}).Prompt
371	}
372	
373	// BuildReviewPromptWithProse builds the review prompt and includes an
374	// advisory prose-quality section derived from the bead text using the same
375	// docprose checker and config path as `ddx doc prose`.
376	func BuildReviewPromptWithProse(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef) string {
377		return BuildReviewPromptBoundedWithProse(b, iter, rev, diff, projectRoot, refs, BuildReviewPromptOptions{Caps: evidence.DefaultCaps()}).Prompt
378	}
379	
380	// BuildReviewPromptBounded assembles the review prompt under byte caps using
381	// the cli/internal/evidence primitives. Governing documents are read via
382	// evidence.ReadFileClamped, the diff is decomposed and clamped via
383	// evidence.ClampDiff, and per-file inlining is bounded by
384	// Caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes. The minimum evidence floor (bead title,
385	// description, acceptance, notes, and the full changed-file inventory) is
386	// preserved verbatim regardless of cap pressure (FEAT-022 §5).
387	func BuildReviewPromptBounded(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef, opts BuildReviewPromptOptions) BuildReviewPromptResult {
388		return buildReviewPromptBounded(b, iter, rev, diff, projectRoot, refs, opts, false)
389	}
390	
391	// BuildReviewPromptBoundedWithProse assembles the review prompt with the
392	// optional prose-quality section enabled.
393	func BuildReviewPromptBoundedWithProse(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef, opts BuildReviewPromptOptions) BuildReviewPromptResult {
394		return buildReviewPromptBounded(b, iter, rev, diff, projectRoot, refs, opts, true)
395	}
396	
397	func buildReviewPromptBounded(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef, opts BuildReviewPromptOptions, includeProse bool) BuildReviewPromptResult {
398		caps := opts.Caps
399		if caps.MaxPromptBytes == 0 {
400			caps = evidence.DefaultCaps()
401		}
402	
403		var sb strings.Builder
404		sections := []evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{}
405	
406		sb.WriteString("<bead-review>\n")
407	
408		// ── Bead section (floor) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
409		beadStart := sb.Len()
410		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <bead id=%q iter=%d>\n", b.ID, iter)
411		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <title>%s</title>\n", reviewXMLEscape(strings.TrimSpace(b.Title)))
412	
413		if desc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Description); desc != "" {
414			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <description>\n%s\n    </description>\n", reviewXMLEscape(desc))
415		} else {
416			sb.WriteString("    <description/>\n")
417		}
418	
419		if acc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Acceptance); acc != "" {
420			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <acceptance>\n%s\n    </acceptance>\n", reviewXMLEscape(acc))
421		} else {
422			sb.WriteString("    <acceptance/>\n")
423		}
424	
425		if notes := strings.TrimSpace(b.Notes); notes != "" {
426			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <notes>\n%s\n    </notes>\n", reviewXMLEscape(notes))
427		}
428	
429		if len(b.Labels) > 0 {
430			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <labels>%s</labels>\n", reviewXMLEscape(strings.Join(b.Labels, ", ")))
431		}
432	
433		sb.WriteString("  </bead>\n\n")
434		sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
435			Name:          "bead",
436			BytesIncluded: sb.Len() - beadStart,
437			SelectedItems: []string{"bead"},
438		})
439	
440		// ── Changed-file inventory (floor) ──────────────────────────────────────
441		allFiles := evidence.DecomposeDiff(diff)
442		if len(allFiles) > 0 {
443			invStart := sb.Len()
444			sb.WriteString("  <changed-files>\n")
445			paths := make([]string, 0, len(allFiles))
446			for _, f := range allFiles {
447				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <file>%s</file>\n", reviewXMLEscape(f.Path))
448				paths = append(paths, f.Path)
449			}
450			sb.WriteString("  </changed-files>\n\n")
451			sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
452				Name:          "changed-files",
453				BytesIncluded: sb.Len() - invStart,
454				SelectedItems: paths,
455			})
456		}
457	
458		// ── AC-Check section (structured mechanical results) ────────────────────
459		if opts.ACCheckJSON != "" {
460			acStart := sb.Len()
461			sb.WriteString("  <ac-check>\n")
462			sb.WriteString(opts.ACCheckJSON)
463			sb.WriteString("\n  </ac-check>\n\n")
464			sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
465				Name:          "ac-check",
466				BytesIncluded: sb.Len() - acStart,
467				SelectedItems: []string{"ac-check.json"},
468			})
469		}
470	
471		// ── Governing docs section (clamped per-doc) ────────────────────────────
472		sb.WriteString("  <governing>\n")
473		if len(refs) == 0 {
474			sb.WriteString("    <note>No governing documents found. Evaluate the diff against the acceptance criteria alone.</note>\n")
475		} else {
476			for _, ref := range refs {
477				if ref.Title != "" {
478					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref id=%q path=%q title=%q>\n", ref.ID, ref.Path, ref.Title)
479				} else {
480					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref id=%q path=%q>\n", ref.ID, ref.Path)
481				}
482				docPath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, filepath.FromSlash(ref.Path))
483				content, truncated, originalBytes, readErr := evidence.ReadFileClamped(docPath, caps.MaxGoverningDocBytes)
484				if readErr != nil {
485					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <note>Could not read %s: %s</note>\n", ref.Path, readErr)
486					sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
487						Name:             "governing:" + ref.ID,
488						TruncationReason: "read_error",
489						OmittedItems:     []string{ref.Path},
490					})
491				} else {
492					txt := strings.TrimSpace(string(content))
493					if truncated {
494						txt += evidence.TruncationMarker
495					}
496					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <content>\n%s\n      </content>\n", evidence.DelimitUntrustedData(txt))
497					sec := evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
498						Name:          "governing:" + ref.ID,
499						BytesIncluded: len(content),
500						SelectedItems: []string{ref.Path},
501					}
502					if truncated {
503						sec.TruncationReason = "governing_doc_cap"
504						sec.BytesOmitted = int(originalBytes) - len(content)
505					}
506					sections = append(sections, sec)
507				}
508				sb.WriteString("    </ref>\n")
509			}
510		}
511		sb.WriteString("  </governing>\n\n")
512	
513		// ── Diff section (ranked, per-file inlined cap, total clamp) ────────────
514		rankedDiff := rankAndDegradeDiffForReview(allFiles, diff, b, refs, caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes)
515		clampedDiff, diffSection := evidence.ClampDiff(rankedDiff, caps.MaxDiffBytes)
516		diffSection.Name = "diff"
517		sections = append(sections, diffSection)
518		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <diff rev=%q>\n%s\n  </diff>\n\n", rev, evidence.DelimitUntrustedData(strings.TrimRight(clampedDiff, "\n")))
519	
520		if includeProse {
521			proseSection, proseSectionEvidence, proseErr := buildProseReviewSection(projectRoot, b)
522			if proseErr != nil {
523				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <prose-review advisory=%q>\n", "true")
524				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <note>Could not compute prose findings: %s</note>\n", reviewXMLEscape(proseErr.Error()))
525				sb.WriteString("  </prose-review>\n\n")
526				sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
527					Name:          "prose-review",
528					SelectedItems: []string{"bead"},
529				})
530			} else {
531				sb.WriteString(proseSection)
532				sb.WriteString("\n")
533				sections = append(sections, proseSectionEvidence)
534			}
535		}
536	
537		// ── Strictness mode section ──────────────────────────────────────────────
538		mode := beadStrictnessMode(b.Labels)
539		modeStart := sb.Len()
540		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <strictness-mode mode=%q>%s</strictness-mode>\n\n", mode, reviewXMLEscape(strictnessModeInstructions(mode)))
541		sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
542			Name:          "strictness-mode",
543			BytesIncluded: sb.Len() - modeStart,
544			SelectedItems: []string{mode},
545		})
546	
547		// ── Instructions section ────────────────────────────────────────────────
548		instructions := strings.ReplaceAll(beadReviewInstructions, "<bead-id>", b.ID)
549		instructions = strings.ReplaceAll(instructions, "<N>", fmt.Sprintf("%d", iter))
550		if includeProse {
551			instructions += "\n\nIf a <prose-review> section is present, add a prose_findings array to the JSON output. Keep prose findings separate from correctness findings. Prose findings are advisory by default and must not change the overall verdict."
552		}
553		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <instructions>\n%s\n  </instructions>\n", reviewXMLEscape(instructions))
554	
555		sb.WriteString("</bead-review>\n")
556	
557		out := sb.String()
558		assembled := evidence.AssembleInline([]evidence.SectionInput{{
559			Name:    "review-prompt",
560			Content: out,
561		}}, len(out))
562		return BuildReviewPromptResult{
563			Prompt:   assembled.Prompt,
564			Overflow: len(out) > caps.MaxPromptBytes,
565			Sections: sections,
566		}
567	}

569	func buildProseReviewSection(projectRoot string, b *bead.Bead) (string, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection, error) {
570		cfg, err := ddxconfig.LoadWithWorkingDir(projectRoot)
571		if err != nil {
572			return "", evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{}, err
573		}
574	
575		settings, err := docprose.ResolveSettings(cfg)
576		if err != nil {
577			return "", evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{}, err
578		}
579		checker, err := docprose.NewChecker(settings.Mode, settings.Vocabulary)
580		if err != nil {
581			return "", evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{}, err
582		}
583	
584		var src strings.Builder
585		if title := strings.TrimSpace(b.Title); title != "" {
586			fmt.Fprintf(&src, "# %s\n\n", title)
587		}
588		if desc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Description); desc != "" {
589			src.WriteString(desc)
590			src.WriteString("\n\n")
591		}
592		if acc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Acceptance); acc != "" {
593			src.WriteString("## Acceptance\n\n")
594			src.WriteString(acc)
595			src.WriteString("\n\n")
596		}
597		if notes := strings.TrimSpace(b.Notes); notes != "" {
598			src.WriteString("## Notes\n\n")
599			src.WriteString(notes)
600			src.WriteString("\n")
601		}
602	
603		findings := checker.Findings("bead.md", src.String())
604		var sb strings.Builder
605		sb.WriteString("  <prose-review advisory=\"true\">\n")
606		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <mode>%s</mode>\n", settings.Mode)
607		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <policy>%s</policy>\n", settings.Policy)
608		if len(findings) == 0 {
609			sb.WriteString("    <findings/>\n")
610		} else {
611			sb.WriteString("    <findings>\n")
612			for _, finding := range findings {
613				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <finding file=%q line=%d rule_id=%q severity=%q>\n", finding.File, finding.Line, finding.RuleID, finding.Severity)
614				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "        <rationale>%s</rationale>\n", reviewXMLEscape(finding.Rationale))
615				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "        <suggested_edit>%s</suggested_edit>\n", reviewXMLEscape(finding.SuggestedEdit))
616				sb.WriteString("      </finding>\n")
617			}
618			sb.WriteString("    </findings>\n")
619		}
620		sb.WriteString("  </prose-review>")
621	
622		section := evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
623			Name:          "prose-review",
624			SelectedItems: []string{"bead"},
625		}
626		section.BytesIncluded = len(src.String())
627		return sb.String(), section, nil
628	}

630	// rankAndDegradeDiffForReview reorders diff files so that AC-referenced files
631	// appear first, then files matching governing-ref paths, then others; and
632	// degrades any individual file body that exceeds maxInlinedFileBytes to
633	// "stat + hunk headers only" so a single oversize file cannot dominate the
634	// diff section. The returned string is a re-serialized unified diff suitable
635	// for evidence.ClampDiff. FEAT-022 §1.
636	func rankAndDegradeDiffForReview(files []evidence.DiffFile, originalDiff string, b *bead.Bead, refs []GoverningRef, maxInlinedFileBytes int) string {
637		if len(files) == 0 {
638			return originalDiff
639		}
640		rank := func(f evidence.DiffFile) int {
641			if f.Path == "" {
642				return 3
643			}
644			if mentionsPathLike(b.Acceptance, f.Path) {
645				return 0
646			}
647			for _, r := range refs {
648				if pathsRelated(r.Path, f.Path) {
649					return 1
650				}
651			}
652			return 2
653		}
654		ordered := make([]evidence.DiffFile, len(files))
655		copy(ordered, files)
656		sort.SliceStable(ordered, func(i, j int) bool { return rank(ordered[i]) < rank(ordered[j]) })
657	
658		var b2 strings.Builder
659		for _, f := range ordered {
660			body := f.Body
661			if maxInlinedFileBytes > 0 && len(body) > maxInlinedFileBytes {
662				body = degradeDiffFileBody(f)
663			}
664			b2.WriteString(body)
665		}
666		return b2.String()
667	}
668	
669	// degradeDiffFileBody returns a stat + hunk-headers only rendering of a diff
670	// file (FEAT-022 §1: large-file degradation). Used when a single file's body
671	// exceeds Caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes — preserves enough structure for the
672	// reviewer to know what changed without inlining the full content.
673	func degradeDiffFileBody(f evidence.DiffFile) string {
674		var b strings.Builder
675		b.WriteString("diff --git a/")
676		b.WriteString(f.Path)
677		b.WriteString(" b/")
678		b.WriteString(f.Path)
679		b.WriteString("\n")
680		if f.Stat != "" {
681			b.WriteString(f.Stat)
682			b.WriteString("\n")
683		}
684		for _, h := range f.HunkHeaders {
685			b.WriteString(h)
686			b.WriteString("\n")
687		}
688		b.WriteString("[…file body omitted by ddx evidence cap…]\n")
689		return b.String()
690	}
691	
692	// mentionsPathLike returns true when text mentions the file path or its
693	// basename (a coarse heuristic — the reviewer only needs ranking, not
694	// exact symbolic resolution).
695	func mentionsPathLike(text, p string) bool {
696		if text == "" || p == "" {
697			return false
698		}
699		if strings.Contains(text, p) {
700			return true
701		}
702		base := path.Base(p)
703		return base != "" && base != "." && strings.Contains(text, base)
704	}
705	
706	// pathsRelated returns true when refPath and filePath share a directory
707	// prefix or basename, used to associate diff files with governing
708	// documents.
709	func pathsRelated(refPath, filePath string) bool {
710		if refPath == "" || filePath == "" {
711			return false
712		}
713		refDir := path.Dir(refPath)
714		if refDir != "" && refDir != "." && strings.HasPrefix(filePath, refDir+"/") {
715			return true
716		}
717		if path.Base(refPath) == path.Base(filePath) {
718			return true
719		}
720		return false
721	}
722	
723	// reviewXMLEscape escapes &, <, and > for inclusion in XML text content.
724	func reviewXMLEscape(s string) string {
725		var buf bytes.Buffer
726		buf.WriteString(strings.NewReplacer(
727			"&", "&",
728			"<", "<",
729			">", ">",
730		).Replace(s))
731		return buf.String()
732	}

734	// DefaultBeadReviewer implements BeadReviewer by dispatching the review
735	// invocation through the agent service (or a test-injected runner).
736	// It fetches the bead, builds the review prompt, and runs the reviewer agent.
737	type DefaultBeadReviewer struct {
738		ProjectRoot string
739		BeadStore   BeadReader
740		// Service, when non-nil, is the agentlib.FizeauService used to dispatch the
741		// review invocation. Production callers leave this nil — ReviewBead
742		// constructs a fresh service from ProjectRoot via NewServiceFromWorkDir.
743		Service agentlib.FizeauService
744		// Runner, when non-nil, replaces the service-based dispatch path. Used by
745		// tests to return canned *Result values without spinning up a real
746		// service. Takes precedence over Service.
747		Runner AgentRunner
748		// Harness and Model override the reviewer harness/model.
749		// When Harness is empty, the reviewer follows the implementation harness;
750		// when both are empty, dispatch is left to the agent service.
751		Harness string
752		Model   string
753		// ModelRef is retained for tests/legacy callers that explicitly want an
754		// upstream model-ref override. Default reviewer routing uses fizeau profiles.
755		ModelRef string
756		// Caps configures the per-section evidence caps used when assembling
757		// the review prompt (FEAT-022). When zero-valued, evidence.DefaultCaps
758		// applies.
759		Caps evidence.Caps
760		// BeadEvents, when non-nil, is used to append the
761		// kind:review-pairing-degraded event when the reviewer's resolved
762		// provider matches the implementer's. Optional: nil disables the
763		// telemetry emission while preserving the rest of the review flow.
764		BeadEvents BeadEventAppender
765		// EventReader, when non-nil, is used to count prior review escalation
766		// triggers (review-error and review-pairing-degraded events) for the
767		// same result_rev so the reviewer MinPower is bumped on retry.
768		EventReader BeadEventReader
769	}

771	// BuildReviewExecuteRequest constructs the AgentRunRuntime used for the
772	// post-merge reviewer dispatch. It sets the reviewer harness/profile,
773	// attaches the implementer's correlation map (with role=reviewer overlaid),
774	// and derives MinPower as impl.ActualPower + 1 so routing is biased toward a
775	// stronger candidate than the implementer's actual selection. The returned
776	// runtime is missing per-call plumbing (Prompt/PromptFile, WorkDir,
777	// SessionLogDirOverride) — the caller fills those in before dispatching.
778	func BuildReviewExecuteRequest(impl ImplementerRouting, reviewerHarness, reviewerProfile string) AgentRunRuntime {
779		return BuildReviewGroupExecuteRequest(impl, reviewerHarness, reviewerProfile, ReviewGroupDispatchMeta{})
780	}
781	
782	// BuildReviewGroupExecuteRequest constructs the reviewer runtime for one
783	// slot in a review group. It threads the implementer's correlation facts,
784	// overlays reviewer role metadata, and stamps the review_group_id and
785	// reviewer_index when provided so downstream routing / tracing can join the
786	// two reviewer slots back to the same evidence bundle.
787	func BuildReviewGroupExecuteRequest(impl ImplementerRouting, reviewerHarness, reviewerProfile string, meta ReviewGroupDispatchMeta) AgentRunRuntime {
788		correlation := map[string]string{}
789		for k, v := range impl.Correlation {
790			correlation[k] = v
791		}
792		correlation["role"] = "reviewer"
793		if meta.GroupID != "" {
794			correlation["review_group_id"] = meta.GroupID
795			correlation["reviewer_index"] = fmt.Sprintf("%d", meta.ReviewerIndex)
796		}
797		if impl.Harness != "" {
798			correlation["impl_harness"] = impl.Harness
799		}
800		if impl.Provider != "" {
801			correlation["impl_provider"] = impl.Provider
802		}
803		if impl.Model != "" {
804			correlation["impl_model"] = impl.Model
805		}
806		if impl.ActualPower > 0 {
807			correlation["impl_actual_power"] = fmt.Sprintf("%d", impl.ActualPower)
808		}
809		minPower := 0
810		if impl.ActualPower > 0 {
811			minPower = impl.ActualPower + 1
812		}
813		correlationID := reviewCorrelationID(correlation)
814		return AgentRunRuntime{
815			HarnessOverride:     reviewerHarness,
816			ProfileOverride:     reviewerProfile,
817			MinPowerOverride:    minPower,
818			Correlation:         correlation,
819			Role:                "reviewer",
820			CorrelationID:       correlationID,
821			PermissionsOverride: PermissionsReadOnlyReviewer,
822			ClearRoutingPins:    true,
823			ClearProfile:        true,
824			ClearMaxPower:       true,
825		}
826	}

828	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) reviewerProfileForDispatch(ctx context.Context, impl ImplementerRouting) string {
829		return r.reviewerDispatchProfile(ctx, impl, 0).Name
830	}
831	
832	// reviewerDispatchProfile returns the fizeau profile name and MinPower to use
833	// for the reviewer dispatch. priorErrors is the number of prior review-error
834	// and review-pairing-degraded events for the same result_rev; each adds one
835	// ladder step to MinPower:
836	//   - 0 prior errors: impl.ActualPower+1 (baseline R4 pairing)
837	//   - 1 prior error:  impl.ActualPower+2 (one step above baseline)
838	//   - 2+ prior errors: top-of-ladder (SelectStrongestProfile, no floor)
839	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) reviewerDispatchProfile(ctx context.Context, impl ImplementerRouting, priorErrors int) reviewerDispatchProfile {
840		floor := 0
841		if impl.ActualPower > 0 {
842			floor = impl.ActualPower + 1
843		}
844		// Escalated floor: each prior error adds one ladder step.
845		escalatedFloor := floor
846		if priorErrors > 0 && floor > 0 {
847			escalatedFloor = floor + priorErrors
848		}
849		if r.Runner != nil {
850			return reviewerDispatchProfile{MinPower: escalatedFloor}
851		}
852		selectedSvc := r.Service
853		if selectedSvc == nil {
854			factory := serviceRunFactory
855			if factory == nil {
856				factory = NewServiceFromWorkDir
857			}
858			built, err := factory(r.ProjectRoot)
859			if err != nil {
860				return reviewerDispatchProfile{MinPower: escalatedFloor}
861			}
862			selectedSvc = built
863		}
864		snap, err := LoadProfileSnapshot(ctx, selectedSvc)
865		if err != nil {
866			return reviewerDispatchProfile{MinPower: escalatedFloor}
867		}
868		// On 2+ prior errors (or 1+ error with unknown impl power), use the
869		// strongest available profile without a floor restriction (top-of-ladder).
870		topOfLadder := priorErrors >= 2 || (priorErrors >= 1 && floor == 0)
871		name := ""
872		if topOfLadder {
873			name = SelectStrongestProfile(snap)
874		} else if escalatedFloor > 0 {
875			name = SelectStrongestProfileAbove(snap, escalatedFloor)
876		} else {
877			name = selectSmartReviewerProfile(snap)
878		}
879		if name == "" {
880			return reviewerDispatchProfile{MinPower: escalatedFloor}
881		}
882		for _, profile := range snap.Profiles {
883			if profile.Name == name {
884				// Use the higher of the profile's own MinPower and the escalated floor
885				// so top-of-ladder selection never yields a MinPower below the floor
886				// we computed from the error count.
887				mp := profile.MinPower
888				if escalatedFloor > mp {
889					mp = escalatedFloor
890				}
891				return reviewerDispatchProfile{Name: name, MinPower: mp}
892			}
893		}
894		return reviewerDispatchProfile{Name: name, MinPower: escalatedFloor}
895	}
896	
897	func selectSmartReviewerProfile(snap ProfileSnapshot) string {
898		for _, profile := range snap.Profiles {
899			if profile.Name == "smart" && profileHasAvailableModel(profile, snap.Models) {
900				return profile.Name
901			}
902		}
903		return SelectStrongestProfile(snap)
904	}

906	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) applyExplicitReviewerPins(runtime *AgentRunRuntime) string {
907		if runtime == nil {
908			return ""
909		}
910		if r.Model != "" {
911			runtime.ModelOverride = r.Model
912			return r.Model
913		}
914		if r.ModelRef != "" {
915			runtime.ModelRefOverride = r.ModelRef
916			return r.ModelRef
917		}
918		return runtime.ProfileOverride
919	}

921	func reviewCorrelationID(correlation map[string]string) string {
922		if len(correlation) == 0 {
923			return ""
924		}
925		parts := make([]string, 0, 4)
926		if beadID := correlation["bead_id"]; beadID != "" {
927			parts = append(parts, beadID)
928		}
929		if groupID := correlation["review_group_id"]; groupID != "" {
930			parts = append(parts, groupID)
931		} else if resultRev := correlation["result_rev"]; resultRev != "" {
932			parts = append(parts, resultRev)
933		}
934		if reviewerIndex := correlation["reviewer_index"]; reviewerIndex != "" {
935			parts = append(parts, reviewerIndex)
936		}
937		if len(parts) == 0 {
938			return ""
939		}
940		return strings.Join(parts, ":")
941	}
942	
943	// reviewPairingDegradedBody renders the kind:review-pairing-degraded event
944	// body. The body carries both implementer and reviewer routing details so
945	// operators can see exactly which routes converged. resultRev is included so
946	// the event can be scoped to a specific commit when counting escalation
947	// triggers.
948	func reviewPairingDegradedBody(impl ImplementerRouting, reviewerHarness, reviewerProvider, reviewerModel string, reviewerPower int, resultRev string) string {
949		var b strings.Builder
950		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "impl_harness=%s\n", impl.Harness)
951		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "impl_provider=%s\n", impl.Provider)
952		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "impl_model=%s\n", impl.Model)
953		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "impl_actual_power=%d\n", impl.ActualPower)
954		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "reviewer_harness=%s\n", reviewerHarness)
955		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "reviewer_provider=%s\n", reviewerProvider)
956		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "reviewer_model=%s\n", reviewerModel)
957		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "reviewer_actual_power=%d\n", reviewerPower)
958		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "min_power_requested=%d\n", impl.ActualPower+1)
959		if resultRev != "" {
960			fmt.Fprintf(&b, "result_rev=%s\n", resultRev)
961		}
962		return b.String()
963	}
964	
965	// countPriorEscalationTriggers counts review-error and review-pairing-degraded
966	// events recorded against the bead whose body cites the given result_rev. Each
967	// counts as one escalation step — review-error events from transport/overflow/
968	// parse failures and pairing-degraded events from routing collisions both
969	// signal that the current reviewer tier is insufficient.
970	func countPriorEscalationTriggers(reader BeadEventReader, beadID, resultRev string) int {
971		if reader == nil || resultRev == "" {
972			return 0
973		}
974		events, err := reader.Events(beadID)
975		if err != nil {
976			return 0
977		}
978		n := 0
979		for _, ev := range events {
980			if ev.Kind != "review-error" && ev.Kind != ReviewPairingDegradedEventKind {
981				continue
982			}
983			m := reResultRevField.FindStringSubmatch(ev.Body)
984			if m == nil || m[1] != resultRev {
985				continue
986			}
987			n++
988		}
989		return n
990	}
991	
992	// reviewerEscalatedEventBody renders the kind:reviewer-escalated event body.
993	func reviewerEscalatedEventBody(newMinPower, errorCount int, resultRev string) string {
994		var b strings.Builder
995		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "new_min_power=%d\n", newMinPower)
996		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "error_count=%d\n", errorCount)
997		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "result_rev=%s\n", resultRev)
998		return b.String()
999	}

1011	// Review implements CandidateReviewer for the pre-land candidate-cycle path.
1012	// It reviews the full base_rev..candidate_rev range and dispatches the reviewer
1013	// inside the still-live attempt worktree.
1014	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) Review(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, candidate CandidateResult) (CandidateReviewResult, error) {
1015		reviewer := *r
1016		if reviewer.ProjectRoot == "" {
1017			reviewer.ProjectRoot = projectRoot
1018		}
1019		if reviewer.ProjectRoot == "" {
1020			return CandidateReviewResult{}, fmt.Errorf("candidate reviewer: project_root required")
1021		}
1022		beadID := candidate.Report.BeadID
1023		if beadID == "" {
1024			return CandidateReviewResult{}, fmt.Errorf("candidate reviewer: bead_id required")
1025		}
1026		diff, err := reviewer.gitDiff(candidate.Report.BaseRev, candidate.Report.ResultRev)
1027		if err != nil {
1028			return CandidateReviewResult{}, fmt.Errorf("candidate reviewer: git diff %s..%s: %w", candidate.Report.BaseRev, candidate.Report.ResultRev, err)
1029		}
1030		workDir := candidate.WorktreePath
1031		if workDir == "" {
1032			workDir = reviewer.ProjectRoot
1033		}
1034		impl := ImplementerRouting{
1035			Harness:     candidate.Report.Harness,
1036			Provider:    candidate.Report.Provider,
1037			Model:       candidate.Report.Model,
1038			ActualPower: candidate.Report.ActualPower,
1039			Correlation: map[string]string{
1040				"bead_id":       beadID,
1041				"attempt_id":    candidate.Report.AttemptID,
1042				"session_id":    candidate.Report.SessionID,
1043				"result_rev":    candidate.Report.ResultRev,
1044				"candidate_ref": candidate.Report.CandidateRef,
1045				"cycle_index":   fmt.Sprintf("%d", candidate.CycleIndex),
1046			},
1047		}
1048		// Read ac-check.json from the still-live worktree so the reviewer can
1049		// ratify mechanical results rather than re-grepping the diff.
1050		acCheckJSON := ""
1051		if candidate.Report.AttemptID != "" && candidate.WorktreePath != "" {
1052			acCheckPath := filepath.Join(candidate.WorktreePath, ExecuteBeadArtifactDir, candidate.Report.AttemptID, "ac-check.json")
1053			// evidence:allow-unbounded — ac-check.json is a small structured file (~1-2 kB)
1054			if data, readErr := os.ReadFile(acCheckPath); readErr == nil {
1055				acCheckJSON = string(data)
1056			}
1057		}
1058		group, groupErr := reviewer.reviewGroupWithDiff(ctx, beadID, candidate.Report.ResultRev, impl, diff, workDir, acCheckJSON)
1059		review, reduceErr := reducePreCloseReviewGroup(group)
1060		if review == nil {
1061			if reduceErr != nil {
1062				return CandidateReviewResult{}, reduceErr
1063			}
1064			return CandidateReviewResult{}, groupErr
1065		}
1066		out := CandidateReviewResult{
1067			Verdict:        string(review.Verdict),
1068			Rationale:      review.Rationale,
1069			PerAC:          append([]ReviewAC(nil), review.PerAC...),
1070			Findings:       append([]Finding(nil), review.Findings...),
1071			Classification: ClassifyReviewFindings(review).Class,
1072		}
1073		if reduceErr != nil {
1074			return out, reduceErr
1075		}
1076		return out, groupErr
1077	}

1079	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) reviewBeadWithDiff(ctx context.Context, beadID, resultRev string, impl ImplementerRouting, diff, reviewWorkDir string) (*ReviewResult, error) {
1080		b, err := r.BeadStore.Get(beadID)
1081		if err != nil {
1082			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: get bead %s: %w", beadID, err)
1083		}
1084	
1085		// Resolve governing document references.
1086		refs := ResolveGoverningRefs(r.ProjectRoot, b)
1087	
1088		// Determine iteration number from bead events.
1089		iter := 1
1090	
1091		// Count prior escalation triggers so reviewerDispatchProfile can bump
1092		// MinPower on retries.
1093		priorErrors := countPriorEscalationTriggers(r.EventReader, beadID, resultRev)
1094	
1095		// Build the review prompt under evidence caps.
1096		caps := r.Caps
1097		if caps.MaxPromptBytes == 0 {
1098			caps = evidence.DefaultCaps()
1099		}
1100		built := BuildReviewPromptBounded(b, iter, resultRev, diff, r.ProjectRoot, refs, BuildReviewPromptOptions{Caps: caps})
1101		prompt := built.Prompt
1102		attemptID := GenerateAttemptID()
1103		artifacts, err := createArtifactBundle(r.ProjectRoot, r.ProjectRoot, attemptID)
1104		if err != nil {
1105			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: create artifact bundle: %w", err)
1106		}
1107		if err := os.WriteFile(artifacts.PromptAbs, []byte(prompt), 0o644); err != nil {
1108			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: write prompt artifact: %w", err)
1109		}
1110	
1111		// Pre-dispatch short-circuit (FEAT-022 §7): if the assembled prompt
1112		// exceeds MaxPromptBytes after all per-section trimming, skip the
1113		// provider dispatch and return a typed review-error: context_overflow.
1114		// The bead is NOT closed — the loop's review-error event-append path
1115		// records the failure and leaves the bead open for retry.
1116		if built.Overflow {
1117			baseRev := resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev)
1118			reviewProfile := r.reviewerDispatchProfile(ctx, impl, priorErrors)
1119			reviewRouteLabel := reviewProfile.Name
1120			if r.Model != "" {
1121				reviewRouteLabel = r.Model
1122			} else if r.ModelRef != "" {
1123				reviewRouteLabel = r.ModelRef
1124			}
1125			overflowTelemetry := &EvidenceAssemblyTelemetry{
1126				Sections:    built.Sections,
1127				InputBytes:  len(prompt),
1128				OutputBytes: 0,
1129				Harness:     r.Harness,
1130				Model:       reviewRouteLabel,
1131			}
1132			reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
1133				Verdict:         VerdictBlock,
1134				Error:           evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
1135				Rationale:       evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
1136				ReviewerHarness: r.Harness,
1137				ReviewerModel:   reviewRouteLabel,
1138				BaseRev:         baseRev,
1139				ResultRev:       resultRev,
1140				ExecutionDir:    artifacts.DirRel,
1141				CostUSD:         0,
1142				InputBytes:      overflowTelemetry.InputBytes,
1143				OutputBytes:     overflowTelemetry.OutputBytes,
1144			}
1145			_ = writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts, reviewArtifactManifest{
1146				Harness:          r.Harness,
1147				Model:            reviewRouteLabel,
1148				BaseRev:          baseRev,
1149				ResultRev:        resultRev,
1150				Verdict:          string(VerdictBlock),
1151				BeadID:           beadID,
1152				ExecutionDir:     artifacts.DirRel,
1153				EvidenceAssembly: overflowTelemetry,
1154			}, reviewArtifactResult{
1155				Verdict:          string(VerdictBlock),
1156				Error:            evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
1157				EvidenceAssembly: overflowTelemetry,
1158			})
1159			return reviewRes, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: PROMPT_BUDGET_EXCEEDED/context_overflow (assembled prompt %d bytes exceeds cap %d; see %s)",
1160				len(prompt), caps.MaxPromptBytes, artifacts.DirRel)
1161		}
1162	
1163		// Resolve reviewer routing. An explicit reviewer harness/model wins as an
1164		// operator override; otherwise DDx sends only a fizeau profile hint plus
1165		// the reviewer MinPower floor.
1166		reviewHarness := r.Harness
1167		reviewProfile := r.reviewerDispatchProfile(ctx, impl, priorErrors)
1168		// Emit reviewer-escalated event when MinPower is bumped above baseline.
1169		if priorErrors > 0 && r.BeadEvents != nil {
1170			_ = r.BeadEvents.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
1171				Kind:      ReviewerEscalatedEventKind,
1172				Summary:   fmt.Sprintf("reviewer escalated to min_power=%d after %d prior error(s)", reviewProfile.MinPower, priorErrors),
1173				Body:      reviewerEscalatedEventBody(reviewProfile.MinPower, priorErrors, resultRev),
1174				Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
1175				CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
1176			})
1177		}
1178	
1179		start := time.Now()
1180		runRuntime := BuildReviewExecuteRequest(impl, reviewHarness, reviewProfile.Name)
1181		// Apply escalated MinPower: use the higher of the base R4 floor and the
1182		// escalated profile floor so retries reach a stronger reviewer tier.
1183		if reviewProfile.MinPower > runRuntime.MinPowerOverride {
1184			runRuntime.MinPowerOverride = reviewProfile.MinPower
1185		}
1186		reviewRouteLabel := r.applyExplicitReviewerPins(&runRuntime)
1187		runRuntime.Prompt = prompt
1188		runRuntime.WorkDir = reviewWorkDir
1189		result, runErr := r.dispatchReviewRun(ctx, runRuntime)
1190	
1191		durationMS := int(time.Since(start).Milliseconds())
1192		if runErr != nil {
1193			// Transport-class failure (FEAT-022 §12): network or provider-side
1194			// error. Surface as a typed review-error so the loop classifies and
1195			// counts it correctly, rather than masquerading as a BLOCK verdict.
1196			transportTelemetry := &EvidenceAssemblyTelemetry{
1197				Sections:    built.Sections,
1198				InputBytes:  len(prompt),
1199				OutputBytes: 0,
1200				ElapsedMS:   durationMS,
1201				Harness:     reviewHarness,
1202				Model:       reviewRouteLabel,
1203			}
1204			reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
1205				Verdict:         VerdictBlock,
1206				Rationale:       runErr.Error(),
1207				Error:           evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport,
1208				ReviewerHarness: reviewHarness,
1209				ReviewerModel:   reviewRouteLabel,
1210				BaseRev:         resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev),
1211				ResultRev:       resultRev,
1212				ExecutionDir:    artifacts.DirRel,
1213				DurationMS:      durationMS,
1214				CostUSD:         resultCost(result),
1215				InputBytes:      transportTelemetry.InputBytes,
1216				OutputBytes:     transportTelemetry.OutputBytes,
1217			}
1218			_ = writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts, reviewArtifactManifest{
1219				Harness:          reviewHarness,
1220				Model:            reviewRouteLabel,
1221				BaseRev:          reviewRes.BaseRev,
1222				ResultRev:        resultRev,
1223				Verdict:          string(reviewRes.Verdict),
1224				BeadID:           beadID,
1225				ExecutionDir:     artifacts.DirRel,
1226				EvidenceAssembly: transportTelemetry,
1227			}, reviewArtifactResult{
1228				Verdict:          string(reviewRes.Verdict),
1229				Rationale:        reviewRes.Rationale,
1230				Error:            reviewRes.Error,
1231				EvidenceAssembly: transportTelemetry,
1232			})
1233			return reviewRes, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: %s: %w", evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport, runErr)
1234		}
1235	
1236		actualHarness := reviewHarness
1237		actualModel := reviewRouteLabel
1238		actualProvider := ""
1239		actualPower := 0
1240		if result != nil {
1241			if result.Harness != "" {
1242				actualHarness = result.Harness
1243			}
1244			if result.Model != "" {
1245				actualModel = result.Model
1246			}
1247			actualProvider = result.Provider
1248			actualPower = result.ActualPower
1249			durationMS = result.DurationMS
1250		}
1251	
1252		output := ""
1253		sessionID := ""
1254		if result != nil {
1255			output = result.Output
1256			sessionID = result.AgentSessionID
1257		}
1258		// Strict JSON parse: replaces the legacy markdown extractor that silently
1259		// pulled "BLOCK" from the prompt's options-header line whenever the model
1260		// echoed it back (the upstream-report regression). On parse error we emit
1261		// a typed review-error class — the execute-loop leaves the bead open for
1262		// retry rather than mis-recording a BLOCK verdict.
1263		parsed, parseErr := ParseReviewVerdict([]byte(output))
1264		var strictVerdict Verdict
1265		var rationale string
1266		var findings []Finding
1267		if parseErr == nil {
1268			strictVerdict = parsed.Verdict
1269			rationale = strings.TrimSpace(parsed.Summary)
1270			findings = parsed.Findings
1271			if rationale == "" && len(findings) > 0 {
1272				parts := make([]string, 0, len(findings))
1273				for _, f := range findings {
1274					line := strings.TrimSpace(f.Summary)
1275					if line == "" {
1276						continue
1277					}
1278					if f.Location != "" {
1279						line = f.Location + ": " + line
1280					}
1281					parts = append(parts, line)
1282				}
1283				rationale = strings.Join(parts, "\n")
1284			}
1285		}
1286		baseRev := resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev)
1287		telemetry := &EvidenceAssemblyTelemetry{
1288			Sections:    built.Sections,
1289			InputBytes:  len(prompt),
1290			OutputBytes: len(output),
1291			ElapsedMS:   durationMS,
1292			Harness:     actualHarness,
1293			Model:       actualModel,
1294		}
1295		reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
1296			Verdict:          strictVerdict,
1297			Rationale:        rationale,
1298			PerAC:            parsed.PerAC,
1299			Findings:         findings,
1300			ProseFindings:    parsed.ProseFindings,
1301			RawOutput:        output,
1302			ReviewerHarness:  actualHarness,
1303			ReviewerModel:    actualModel,
1304			ReviewerProvider: actualProvider,
1305			SessionID:        sessionID,
1306			BaseRev:          baseRev,
1307			ResultRev:        resultRev,
1308			ExecutionDir:     artifacts.DirRel,
1309			DurationMS:       durationMS,
1310			CostUSD:          resultCost(result),
1311			InputBytes:       telemetry.InputBytes,
1312			OutputBytes:      telemetry.OutputBytes,
1313		}

1315		// R4 pairing-degradation telemetry: when MinPower=actualPower+1 fails
1316		// to push the reviewer onto a different provider than the implementer,
1317		// surface a kind:review-pairing-degraded event so operators can see the
1318		// regression. The result_rev is included in the body so the event can be
1319		// counted as an escalation trigger for subsequent dispatches.

1323				Kind:      ReviewPairingDegradedEventKind,
1324				Summary:   fmt.Sprintf("reviewer pinned to same provider as implementer (%s)", impl.Provider),
1325				Body:      reviewPairingDegradedBody(impl, actualHarness, actualProvider, actualModel, actualPower, resultRev),

1331			Harness:          actualHarness,
1332			Model:            actualModel,
1333			SessionID:        sessionID,

1335			ResultRev:        resultRev,
1336			Verdict:          string(strictVerdict),
1337			BeadID:           beadID,
1338			ExecutionDir:     artifacts.DirRel,
1339			EvidenceAssembly: telemetry,
1340		}, reviewArtifactResult{
1341			Verdict:          string(strictVerdict),
1342			Rationale:        rationale,
1343			PerAC:            parsed.PerAC,
1344			Findings:         findings,
1345			ProseFindings:    parsed.ProseFindings,
1346			Error:            reviewRes.Error,

1348		})

1351			// unparseable (text without a recognizable JSON contract). Operators

1353			// window or backend availability issue, while unparseable signals a
1354			// reviewer-prompt or output-format drift.
1355			class := evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable

1365	// dispatchReviewRun is a thin SD-024 wrapper around dispatchViaResolvedConfig
1366	// for the post-merge reviewer. The reviewer carries no persistent
1367	// ResolvedConfig of its own — the durable knobs that affect a review
1368	// invocation (timeout, provider, evidence caps) are read from the project's
1369	// .ddx/config.yaml via LoadAndResolve, while reviewer-tier harness/model
1370	// are pinned via the runtime override fields. Resolution order matches the
1371	// execute-bead worker (runner > pre-built service > fresh service).
1372	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) dispatchReviewRun(ctx context.Context, runtime AgentRunRuntime) (*Result, error) {
1373		rcfg, _ := ddxconfig.LoadAndResolve(r.ProjectRoot, ddxconfig.CLIOverrides{})
1374		return dispatchViaResolvedConfig(ctx, r.ProjectRoot, r.Service, r.Runner, rcfg, runtime)
1375	}

1377	// gitShow runs `git show <rev>` with pathspec exclusions for execution-
1378	// evidence noise so the review prompt's <diff> section stays bounded even
1379	// when an old commit tracked a multi-thousand-line session log. See
1380	// EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs and ddx-39e27896 for the regression.
1381	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) gitShow(rev string) (string, error) {
1382		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 60*time.Second)
1383		defer cancel()
1384		args := append([]string{"show", rev, "--", "."}, EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs()...)
1385		out, err := internalgit.Command(ctx, r.ProjectRoot, args...).Output()
1386		if err != nil {
1387			return "", err
1388		}
1389		return string(out), nil
1390	}
1391	
1392	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) gitDiff(baseRev, resultRev string) (string, error) {
1393		if strings.TrimSpace(baseRev) == "" || strings.TrimSpace(resultRev) == "" {
1394			return "", fmt.Errorf("base_rev and result_rev are required")
1395		}
1396		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 60*time.Second)
1397		defer cancel()
1398		args := append([]string{"diff", baseRev, resultRev, "--", "."}, EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs()...)
1399		out, err := internalgit.Command(ctx, r.ProjectRoot, args...).Output()
1400		if err != nil {
1401			return "", err
1402		}
1403		return string(out), nil
1404	}
1405	
1406	func resolveReviewBaseRev(projectRoot, resultRev string) string {
1407		if resultRev == "" {
1408			return ""
1409		}
1410		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "rev-parse", resultRev+"^").Output()
1411		if err != nil {
1412			return ""
1413		}
1414		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
1415	}
1416	
1417	func writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts *executeBeadArtifacts, manifest reviewArtifactManifest, result reviewArtifactResult) error {
1418		if artifacts == nil {
1419			return nil
1420		}
1421		if err := writeArtifactJSON(artifacts.ManifestAbs, manifest); err != nil {
1422			return err
1423		}
1424		return writeArtifactJSON(artifacts.ResultAbs, result)
1425	}
1426	
1427	func resultCost(result *Result) float64 {
1428		if result == nil {
1429			return 0
1430		}
1431		return result.CostUSD
1432	}

55	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkAnalyzeCommand() *cobra.Command {
56		cmd := &cobra.Command{
57			Use:   "analyze",
58			Short: "Query cross-attempt performance from .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl",
59			Long: `Aggregate and analyze attempt data from .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl.
60	
61	Group rows by one or more dimensions (--by) and compute aggregated metrics.
62	Filter with --since (time window) or --where (field equality).
63	
64	Supported dimensions: model, harness, profile, prompt_template_hash, spec_id,
65	outcome, bead_id, day, week, month.
66	
67	Examples:
68	  ddx work analyze --by model
69	  ddx work analyze --by model,outcome
70	  ddx work analyze --by spec_id --since 7d
71	  ddx work analyze --where model=claude-sonnet-4-6 --by prompt_template_hash`,
72			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
73			RunE: f.runWorkAnalyze,
74		}
75		cmd.Flags().StringSlice("by", nil, "Dimensions to group by (comma-separated): "+strings.Join(analyzeValidDims, ", "))
76		cmd.Flags().String("since", "", "Time window: e.g. 7d, 2w, 1m (days/weeks/months)")
77		cmd.Flags().StringArray("where", nil, "Filter by key=value; may be repeated")
78		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Emit JSONL output")
79		cmd.Flags().Bool("csv", false, "Emit CSV output")
80		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root (default: CWD git root)")
81		return cmd
82	}
83	
84	func (f *CommandFactory) runWorkAnalyze(cmd *cobra.Command, _ []string) error {
85		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
86		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
87	
88		byDims, _ := cmd.Flags().GetStringSlice("by")
89		sinceStr, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("since")
90		whereFilters, _ := cmd.Flags().GetStringArray("where")
91		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
92		asCSV, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("csv")
93	
94		for _, d := range byDims {
95			if !analyzeDimValid(d) {
96				return fmt.Errorf("unsupported dimension %q; valid: %s", d, strings.Join(analyzeValidDims, ", "))
97			}
98		}
99	
100		var cutoff time.Time
101		if sinceStr != "" {
102			dur, err := analyzeParseSince(sinceStr)
103			if err != nil {
104				return err
105			}
106			cutoff = time.Now().UTC().Add(-dur)
107		}

50	func (f *CommandFactory) newAgentMetricsTierSuccessCommand() *cobra.Command {
51		cmd := &cobra.Command{
52			Use:   "tier-success",
53			Short: "Report success rate per harness/model tier from execution evidence",
54			Long: `Scan .ddx/executions/*/result.json and aggregate execution outcomes
55	into per-tier success rates. A tier is identified by harness alone when the
56	result has no model field, or by harness/model when a concrete model is
57	recorded. Success is defined as outcome == "task_succeeded".`,
58			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
59			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
60				lastN, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("last")
61				jsonOut, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
62	
63				rows, err := computeTierSuccess(f.WorkingDir, lastN)
64				if err != nil {
65					return err
66				}
67	
68				if jsonOut {
69					enc := json.NewEncoder(cmd.OutOrStdout())
70					enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
71					return enc.Encode(rows)
72				}
73				return renderTierSuccessTable(cmd, rows)
74			},
75		}
76		cmd.Flags().Int("last", 0, "Limit to most recent N attempts (0 = all)")
77		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output JSON")
78		return cmd
79	}

81	// computeTierSuccess scans .ddx/executions/*/result.json under workingDir and
82	// returns per-tier aggregates. When lastN > 0, only the most recent lastN
83	// attempts (sorted by attempt directory name, which is a sortable timestamp)
84	// are considered.
85	func computeTierSuccess(workingDir string, lastN int) ([]tierSuccessRow, error) {
86		execRoot := filepath.Join(workingDir, ".ddx", "executions")
87		entries, err := os.ReadDir(execRoot)
88		if err != nil {
89			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
90				return []tierSuccessRow{}, nil
91			}
92			return nil, fmt.Errorf("read executions dir: %w", err)
93		}
94	
95		names := make([]string, 0, len(entries))
96		for _, entry := range entries {
97			if entry.IsDir() {
98				names = append(names, entry.Name())
99			}
100		}
101		// Directory names are sortable timestamps (YYYYMMDDTHHMMSS-<hash>), so
102		// lexicographic sort is chronological.
103		sort.Strings(names)
104	
105		// First pass: read and keep only usable results, preserving chronological
106		// order. --last N then picks the most recent N usable attempts so that
107		// malformed or missing files never hide a valid recent attempt.
108		type loadedResult struct {
109			harness     string
110			model       string
111			outcome     string
112			failureMode string
113			costUSD     float64
114			durMS       int
115		}
116		loaded := make([]loadedResult, 0, len(names))
117		for _, name := range names {
118			resultPath := filepath.Join(execRoot, name, "result.json")
119			raw, err := os.ReadFile(resultPath)
120			if err != nil {
121				continue
122			}
123			var res agent.ExecuteBeadResult
124			if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &res); err != nil {
125				continue
126			}
127			if res.Harness == "" {
128				continue
129			}
130			loaded = append(loaded, loadedResult{
131				harness:     res.Harness,
132				model:       res.Model,
133				outcome:     res.Outcome,
134				failureMode: res.FailureMode,
135				costUSD:     res.CostUSD,
136				durMS:       res.DurationMS,
137			})
138		}
139		if lastN > 0 && len(loaded) > lastN {
140			loaded = loaded[len(loaded)-lastN:]
141		}
142	
143		type agg struct {
144			harness          string
145			model            string
146			attempts         int
147			successes        int
148			totalCostUSD     float64
149			wastedCostUSD    float64
150			effectiveCostUSD float64

262	}
263	
264	// beadReviewInstructions is the review contract embedded in the prompt.
265	// The reviewing agent must produce a single JSON object matching the
266	// ReviewVerdict schema (schema_version: 1). The markdown contract this
267	// replaces silently mis-parsed `### Verdict: APPROVE` outputs whenever the
268	// model echoed the prompt's options header — see review_verdict.go.
269	const beadReviewInstructions = `You are reviewing a bead implementation against its acceptance criteria.
270	
271	## AC-Check Ratification
272	
273	When an <ac-check> section is present, ratify the mechanical results rather
274	than re-verifying them independently from the diff:
275	
276	- result="pass": confirm the evidence is credible. Override to fail only if
277	  the evidence is fabricated — include judgment_override_reason and a diff
278	  citation (file:line) in the per_ac evidence string.
279	- result="fail": mechanically verified failure. Grade as fail and BLOCK unless
280	  the commit message contains an explicit AC-Waive trailer for this AC.
281	- result="needs_judgment": adjudicate from the diff. If you cannot determine
282	  pass/fail without additional bead context from the operator, use
283	  REQUEST_CLARIFICATION for that AC item.
284	- result="error": treat as needs_judgment.
285	
286	Overriding a mechanical grade (pass→fail or fail→pass) requires an explicit
287	judgment_override_reason note and a concrete diff citation in the evidence.
288	
289	## Strictness Mode
290	
291	The <strictness-mode> tag specifies per-bead evidence requirements:
292	
293	- strict (kind:fix, kind:feat): each AC must be anchored to a named Test*
294	  function or a diff-touched symbol; file-only evidence is insufficient.
295	- behavior-light (kind:refactor, kind:chore): build green plus file/symbol
296	  evidence suffices; test-name match required only when an AC explicitly
297	  names a Test* function.
298	- mechanical (kind:doc, kind:mechanical): file presence, renames, or symbol
299	  evidence only; no test-name or runtime evidence required.

339	// BuildReviewPromptOptions configures evidence-bounded prompt assembly.
340	// FEAT-022 §5/§7: caps drive per-section trimming and the pre-dispatch
341	// short-circuit on residual oversize.
342	type BuildReviewPromptOptions struct {
343		Caps evidence.Caps
344		// ACCheckJSON, when non-empty, is the raw JSON content of the
345		// .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ac-check.json file produced by
346		// `ddx bead ac-check`. Injected as a structured <ac-check> section so
347		// the reviewer ratifies mechanical results rather than re-grepping the diff.

356	// BuildReviewPromptResult is the structured output of BuildReviewPromptBounded.
357	// Overflow is true when, after all per-section trimming, the assembled
358	// prompt still exceeds Caps.MaxPromptBytes; callers MUST NOT dispatch in
359	// that case (FEAT-022 §7).
360	type BuildReviewPromptResult struct {
361		Prompt   string
362		Overflow bool
363		Sections []evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection
364	}

366	// BuildReviewPrompt builds the complete review prompt for a bead implementation
367	// using default caps. Callers needing overflow detection use

369	func BuildReviewPrompt(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef) string {

378	}

380	// BuildReviewPromptBounded assembles the review prompt under byte caps using
381	// the cli/internal/evidence primitives. Governing documents are read via
382	// evidence.ReadFileClamped, the diff is decomposed and clamped via
383	// evidence.ClampDiff, and per-file inlining is bounded by
384	// Caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes. The minimum evidence floor (bead title,
385	// description, acceptance, notes, and the full changed-file inventory) is
386	// preserved verbatim regardless of cap pressure (FEAT-022 §5).
387	func BuildReviewPromptBounded(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef, opts BuildReviewPromptOptions) BuildReviewPromptResult {

397	func buildReviewPromptBounded(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef, opts BuildReviewPromptOptions, includeProse bool) BuildReviewPromptResult {
398		caps := opts.Caps
399		if caps.MaxPromptBytes == 0 {
400			caps = evidence.DefaultCaps()
401		}
402	
403		var sb strings.Builder
404		sections := []evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{}
405	
406		sb.WriteString("<bead-review>\n")
407	
408		// ── Bead section (floor) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
409		beadStart := sb.Len()
410		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <bead id=%q iter=%d>\n", b.ID, iter)
411		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <title>%s</title>\n", reviewXMLEscape(strings.TrimSpace(b.Title)))
412	
413		if desc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Description); desc != "" {
414			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <description>\n%s\n    </description>\n", reviewXMLEscape(desc))
415		} else {
416			sb.WriteString("    <description/>\n")
417		}
418	
419		if acc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Acceptance); acc != "" {
420			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <acceptance>\n%s\n    </acceptance>\n", reviewXMLEscape(acc))
421		} else {
422			sb.WriteString("    <acceptance/>\n")
423		}
424	
425		if notes := strings.TrimSpace(b.Notes); notes != "" {
426			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <notes>\n%s\n    </notes>\n", reviewXMLEscape(notes))
427		}
428	
429		if len(b.Labels) > 0 {
430			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <labels>%s</labels>\n", reviewXMLEscape(strings.Join(b.Labels, ", ")))
431		}
432	
433		sb.WriteString("  </bead>\n\n")
434		sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
435			Name:          "bead",
436			BytesIncluded: sb.Len() - beadStart,
437			SelectedItems: []string{"bead"},
438		})
439	
440		// ── Changed-file inventory (floor) ──────────────────────────────────────
441		allFiles := evidence.DecomposeDiff(diff)
442		if len(allFiles) > 0 {
443			invStart := sb.Len()
444			sb.WriteString("  <changed-files>\n")
445			paths := make([]string, 0, len(allFiles))
446			for _, f := range allFiles {
447				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <file>%s</file>\n", reviewXMLEscape(f.Path))
448				paths = append(paths, f.Path)
449			}
450			sb.WriteString("  </changed-files>\n\n")
451			sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
452				Name:          "changed-files",
453				BytesIncluded: sb.Len() - invStart,
454				SelectedItems: paths,
455			})
456		}
457	
458		// ── AC-Check section (structured mechanical results) ────────────────────
459		if opts.ACCheckJSON != "" {
460			acStart := sb.Len()
461			sb.WriteString("  <ac-check>\n")
462			sb.WriteString(opts.ACCheckJSON)
463			sb.WriteString("\n  </ac-check>\n\n")
464			sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
465				Name:          "ac-check",
466				BytesIncluded: sb.Len() - acStart,
467				SelectedItems: []string{"ac-check.json"},
468			})
469		}
470	
471		// ── Governing docs section (clamped per-doc) ────────────────────────────
472		sb.WriteString("  <governing>\n")
473		if len(refs) == 0 {
474			sb.WriteString("    <note>No governing documents found. Evaluate the diff against the acceptance criteria alone.</note>\n")
475		} else {
476			for _, ref := range refs {
477				if ref.Title != "" {
478					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref id=%q path=%q title=%q>\n", ref.ID, ref.Path, ref.Title)
479				} else {
480					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref id=%q path=%q>\n", ref.ID, ref.Path)
481				}
482				docPath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, filepath.FromSlash(ref.Path))
483				content, truncated, originalBytes, readErr := evidence.ReadFileClamped(docPath, caps.MaxGoverningDocBytes)
484				if readErr != nil {

486					sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
487						Name:             "governing:" + ref.ID,
488						TruncationReason: "read_error",
489						OmittedItems:     []string{ref.Path},
490					})
491				} else {

495					}
496					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <content>\n%s\n      </content>\n", evidence.DelimitUntrustedData(txt))
497					sec := evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{

499						BytesIncluded: len(content),

1	// Package attemptmetrics implements the cold-tier metrics layer: one row per
2	// bead-attempt written to .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl. Rows are appended with
3	// O_APPEND so concurrent single-writer access is safe. The schema_version field
4	// allows future migrations without losing historical rows.
5	package attemptmetrics
6	
7	import (
8		"bufio"
9		"encoding/json"
10		"os"
11		"path/filepath"
12		"time"
13	
14		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
15	)
16	
17	const (
18		SchemaVersion   = 1
19		attemptsRelPath = ".ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl"
20	)
21	
22	// AttemptRow is one row in .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl.
23	type AttemptRow struct {
24		SchemaVersion int    `json:"schema_version"`
25		AttemptID     string `json:"attempt_id"`
26		BeadID        string `json:"bead_id"`
27		SessionID     string `json:"session_id,omitempty"`
28		TSStart       string `json:"ts_start,omitempty"` // RFC3339
29		TSEnd         string `json:"ts_end,omitempty"`   // RFC3339
30	
31		Model    string `json:"model,omitempty"`
32		Harness  string `json:"harness,omitempty"`
33		Profile  string `json:"profile,omitempty"`
34		Provider string `json:"provider,omitempty"`
35	
36		// PromptTemplateID and PromptTemplateHash identify the prompt variant used.
37		PromptTemplateID   string `json:"prompt_template_id,omitempty"`
38		PromptTemplateHash string `json:"prompt_template_hash,omitempty"` // hex sha256 of rendered prompt
39	
40		SpecID string `json:"spec_id,omitempty"`
41	
42		Outcome  string  `json:"outcome"`
43		ExitCode int     `json:"exit_code"`
44		CostUSD  float64 `json:"cost_usd,omitempty"`
45	
46		DurationMS   int `json:"duration_ms"`
47		InputTokens  int `json:"input_tokens,omitempty"`
48		OutputTokens int `json:"output_tokens,omitempty"`
49		TotalTokens  int `json:"total_tokens,omitempty"`
50	
51		ReviewVerdict                string         `json:"review_verdict,omitempty"`
52		ReviewFindingCountBySeverity map[string]int `json:"review_finding_count_by_severity,omitempty"`
53	
54		LadderMinPowerInitial int `json:"ladder_min_power_initial,omitempty"`
55		LadderMinPowerFinal   int `json:"ladder_min_power_final,omitempty"`
56		LadderStepsTaken      int `json:"ladder_steps_taken,omitempty"`
57	}
58	
59	// BeadAttemptEvents is the raw input for BackfillFromEvents: a bead ID, its
60	// optional spec-id, and its full event stream.
61	type BeadAttemptEvents struct {
62		BeadID string
63		SpecID string // from bead Extra["spec-id"], optional
64		Events []bead.BeadEvent
65	}
66	
67	// costEventBody matches the JSON shape written by appendBeadCostEvidence.
68	type costEventBody struct {
69		AttemptID    string  `json:"attempt_id"`
70		Harness      string  `json:"harness,omitempty"`
71		Provider     string  `json:"provider,omitempty"`
72		Model        string  `json:"model,omitempty"`
73		InputTokens  int     `json:"input_tokens"`
74		OutputTokens int     `json:"output_tokens"`
75		TotalTokens  int     `json:"total_tokens"`
76		CostUSD      float64 `json:"cost_usd"`
77		DurationMS   int     `json:"duration_ms"`
78		ExitCode     int     `json:"exit_code"`
79	}
80	
81	// AttemptsPath returns the absolute path to .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl
82	// under projectRoot.
83	func AttemptsPath(projectRoot string) string {
84		return filepath.Join(projectRoot, attemptsRelPath)
85	}
86	
87	// AppendRow appends row to .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl under projectRoot using
88	// O_APPEND so concurrent single-writer appends are safe. Creates parent
89	// directories if needed. Best-effort callers may discard the returned error.
90	func AppendRow(projectRoot string, row AttemptRow) error {
91		path := AttemptsPath(projectRoot)
92		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o755); err != nil {
93			return err
94		}
95		f, err := os.OpenFile(path, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_APPEND, 0o644)
96		if err != nil {
97			return err
98		}
99		defer f.Close() //nolint:errcheck
100		data, err := json.Marshal(row)
101		if err != nil {
102			return err
103		}
104		data = append(data, '\n')
105		_, err = f.Write(data)
106		return err
107	}
108	
109	// LoadAttemptIDs reads .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl and returns the set of
110	// attempt_ids already present. Used to deduplicate BackfillFromEvents runs.
111	// Returns an empty map when the file does not exist.
112	func LoadAttemptIDs(projectRoot string) (map[string]struct{}, error) {
113		path := AttemptsPath(projectRoot)
114		f, err := os.Open(path)
115		if err != nil {
116			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
117				return map[string]struct{}{}, nil
118			}
119			return nil, err
120		}
121		defer f.Close() //nolint:errcheck
122	
123		seen := map[string]struct{}{}
124		sc := bufio.NewScanner(f)
125		sc.Buffer(make([]byte, 1024*1024), 1024*1024) // 1 MiB lines max
126		for sc.Scan() {
127			var row struct {
128				AttemptID string `json:"attempt_id"`
129			}
130			if err := json.Unmarshal(sc.Bytes(), &row); err != nil || row.AttemptID == "" {
131				continue
132			}
133			seen[row.AttemptID] = struct{}{}
134		}
135		return seen, sc.Err()
136	}
137	
138	// LoadRows reads all rows from .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl. Malformed lines
139	// are skipped. Returns an empty slice when the file does not exist.
140	func LoadRows(projectRoot string) ([]AttemptRow, error) {
141		path := AttemptsPath(projectRoot)
142		f, err := os.Open(path)
143		if err != nil {
144			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
145				return []AttemptRow{}, nil
146			}
147			return nil, err
148		}
149		defer f.Close() //nolint:errcheck
150	
151		var rows []AttemptRow
152		sc := bufio.NewScanner(f)
153		sc.Buffer(make([]byte, 1024*1024), 1024*1024)
154		for sc.Scan() {
155			var row AttemptRow
156			if err := json.Unmarshal(sc.Bytes(), &row); err != nil {
157				continue
158			}
159			rows = append(rows, row)
160		}
161		return rows, sc.Err()
162	}
163	
164	// BackfillFromEvents processes beads' event streams, appending one AttemptRow
165	// per attempt found (identified by kind:cost events). Deduplicates by
166	// attempt_id: rows already in attempts.jsonl are skipped. Returns the count of
167	// new rows added.
168	//
169	// The matching algorithm: for each kind:cost event, scan forward to find the
170	// next kind:execute-bead event (which carries the finalization outcome). This
171	// pair gives the attempt's cost/token/timing data and its loop-level outcome.
172	func BackfillFromEvents(projectRoot string, beads []BeadAttemptEvents) (int, error) {
173		seen, err := LoadAttemptIDs(projectRoot)
174		if err != nil {
175			return 0, err
176		}
177	
178		var added int
179		for _, b := range beads {
180			rows := extractRowsFromEvents(b)
181			for _, row := range rows {
182				if _, exists := seen[row.AttemptID]; exists {
183					continue
184				}
185				if err := AppendRow(projectRoot, row); err != nil {
186					return added, err
187				}
188				seen[row.AttemptID] = struct{}{}
189				added++
190			}
191		}
192		return added, nil
193	}
194	
195	// extractRowsFromEvents derives one AttemptRow per kind:cost event found in
196	// b.Events. Each cost event is paired with the immediately following
197	// kind:execute-bead event to obtain the outcome.
198	func extractRowsFromEvents(b BeadAttemptEvents) []AttemptRow {
199		var rows []AttemptRow
200		for i, ev := range b.Events {
201			if ev.Kind != "cost" {
202				continue
203			}
204			var cost costEventBody
205			if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(ev.Body), &cost); err != nil || cost.AttemptID == "" {
206				continue
207			}
208	
209			// Scan forward for the next execute-bead event to get outcome.
210			outcome := ""
211			for _, next := range b.Events[i+1:] {
212				if next.Kind == "execute-bead" {
213					outcome = next.Summary
214					break
215				}
216			}
217	
218			tsEnd := ""
219			if !ev.CreatedAt.IsZero() {
220				tsEnd = ev.CreatedAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
221			}
222			// Approximate ts_start from ts_end and duration.
223			tsStart := ""
224			if !ev.CreatedAt.IsZero() && cost.DurationMS > 0 {
225				start := ev.CreatedAt.UTC().Add(-time.Duration(cost.DurationMS) * time.Millisecond)
226				tsStart = start.Format(time.RFC3339)
227			}
228	
229			total := cost.TotalTokens
230			if total == 0 {
231				total = cost.InputTokens + cost.OutputTokens
232			}
233	
234			row := AttemptRow{
235				SchemaVersion: SchemaVersion,
236				AttemptID:     cost.AttemptID,
237				BeadID:        b.BeadID,
238				SpecID:        b.SpecID,
239				TSStart:       tsStart,
240				TSEnd:         tsEnd,
241				Model:         cost.Model,
242				Harness:       cost.Harness,
243				Provider:      cost.Provider,
244				Outcome:       outcome,
245				ExitCode:      cost.ExitCode,
246				CostUSD:       cost.CostUSD,
247				DurationMS:    cost.DurationMS,
248				InputTokens:   cost.InputTokens,
249				OutputTokens:  cost.OutputTokens,
250				TotalTokens:   total,
251			}
252			rows = append(rows, row)
253		}
254		return rows
255	}
256	
257	// Rfc3339 formats a time.Time as RFC3339 UTC; returns "" for the zero value.
258	func Rfc3339(t time.Time) string {
259		if t.IsZero() {
260			return ""
261		}
262		return t.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
263	}
264
