9	// Bead represents a portable work item with metadata.
10	// The schema matches bd/br JSONL format for interchange compatibility.
11	// Unknown fields from external sources are preserved in Extra.
12	type Bead struct {
13		ID        string    `json:"id"`
14		Title     string    `json:"title"`
15		Status    string    `json:"status"`
16		Priority  int       `json:"priority"`
17		IssueType string    `json:"issue_type"`
18		Owner     string    `json:"owner,omitempty"`
19		CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
20		CreatedBy string    `json:"created_by,omitempty"`
21		UpdatedAt time.Time `json:"updated_at"`
22	
23		// Optional fields (bd-compatible)
24		Labels      []string `json:"labels,omitempty"`
25		Parent      string   `json:"parent,omitempty"`
26		Description string   `json:"description,omitempty"`
27		Acceptance  string   `json:"acceptance,omitempty"`
28		Notes       string   `json:"notes,omitempty"`
29	
30		// Dependencies use bd-compatible format
31		Dependencies []Dependency `json:"dependencies,omitempty"`
32	
33		// Extra holds unknown fields for round-trip preservation.
34		// Workflow-specific fields (e.g. HELIX spec-id, execution-eligible)
35		// are stored here and written back on save.
36		Extra map[string]any `json:"-"`
37	}

39	// Dependency represents a link between two beads (bd-compatible format).
40	type Dependency struct {
41		IssueID     string `json:"issue_id"`
42		DependsOnID string `json:"depends_on_id"`
43		Type        string `json:"type"` // "blocks", "related", etc.
44		CreatedAt   string `json:"created_at,omitempty"`
45		CreatedBy   string `json:"created_by,omitempty"`
46		Metadata    string `json:"metadata,omitempty"`
47	}

54		Actor     string    `json:"actor,omitempty"`

56		Source    string    `json:"source,omitempty"`
57	}

59	// Status constants
60	const (
61		StatusOpen       = "open"
62		StatusInProgress = "in_progress"
63		StatusClosed     = "closed"
64		// StatusBlocked marks an explicit external blocker that can be rechecked.
65		// Dependency waits are derived from dependency status and do not persist as
66		// blocked.
67		StatusBlocked = "blocked"
68		// StatusProposed marks work awaiting operator approval or clarification.
69		// It is outside worker dispatch until transitioned to open or cancelled.
70		StatusProposed = "proposed"
71		// StatusCancelled marks work that will not run and does not satisfy
72		// dependents.
73		StatusCancelled = "cancelled"
74	)

76	// CanonicalStatuses is the single source of truth for the persisted bead
77	// status enumeration. It mirrors the bd/br canonical set documented in
78	// TD-031 §2 and the JSON Schema enum at
79	// cli/internal/bead/schema/bead-record.schema.json. The CI guard tests in
80	// sync_test.go assert that schema, TD doc, and Go source agree with this
81	// list; adding or removing a value here without updating the schema and TD
82	// will fail CI.
83	var CanonicalStatuses = []string{
84		StatusOpen,
85		StatusInProgress,
86		StatusClosed,
87		StatusBlocked,
88		StatusProposed,
89		StatusCancelled,
90	}
91	
92	// IsCanonicalStatus reports whether s is one of the persisted bead statuses
93	// in CanonicalStatuses.
94	func IsCanonicalStatus(s string) bool {
95		for _, c := range CanonicalStatuses {
96			if c == s {
97				return true
98			}
99		}
100		return false
101	}

103	// IssueType constants
104	const (
105		// IssueTypeOperatorPrompt is the bead type used for operator-submitted
106		// prompts that the execute-loop runs as instructions.
107		IssueTypeOperatorPrompt = "operator-prompt"
108	)
109	
110	// Default labels and other defaults for operator-prompt beads.
111	const (
112		OperatorPromptLabelKind   = "kind:operator-prompt"
113		OperatorPromptLabelSource = "source:web-ui"
114		// OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance is the auto-AC stub used when an
115		// operator submits a prompt without explicit acceptance criteria. The
116		// structural AC verifier is skipped for operator-prompt beads, so this
117		// stub stands as a human-readable contract only.
118		OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance = "Agent must produce a diff or no_changes rationale; the prompt body is the contract."
119	)
120	
121	// OperatorPromptMutationGuard enforces the no-self-mutation rule from
122	// Story 15: an operator-prompt bead's execution may not create, edit, or
123	// close another operator-prompt bead. The guard returns nil when the
124	// mutation is allowed and a non-nil error when it must be rejected.
125	//
126	// actorIssueType is the issue_type of the bead currently being executed
127	// (empty when no operator-prompt context is active). targetIssueType is
128	// the issue_type of the bead about to be mutated.
129	//
130	// Allow/deny matrix:
131	//
132	//	actor=""               , target=*                → allow
133	//	actor="task"           , target=*                → allow
134	//	actor="operator-prompt", target!="operator-prompt" → allow
135	//	actor="operator-prompt", target=="operator-prompt" → deny
136	func OperatorPromptMutationGuard(actorIssueType, targetIssueType string) error {
137		if actorIssueType != IssueTypeOperatorPrompt {
138			return nil
139		}
140		if targetIssueType != IssueTypeOperatorPrompt {
141			return nil
142		}
143		return fmt.Errorf("bead: operator-prompt bead may not mutate another operator-prompt bead")
144	}
145	
146	// NewOperatorPromptBead constructs a fresh operator-prompt bead from a raw
147	// prompt string, applying the Story 15 template defaults: title is the first
148	// non-empty line of the prompt, full prompt body is preserved verbatim in the
149	// description, default labels are kind:operator-prompt + source:web-ui, the
150	// status starts in `proposed` (approval flow), priority defaults to the
151	// caller-supplied tier (clamped to MinPriority..MaxPriority), and the
152	// acceptance field carries the auto-AC stub.
153	//
154	// The returned bead is not persisted; callers feed it to Store.Create which
155	// will assign the ID and CreatedAt/UpdatedAt timestamps.
156	func NewOperatorPromptBead(prompt string, defaultTier int) *Bead {
157		body := strings.TrimSpace(prompt)
158		title := body
159		if i := strings.IndexByte(body, '\n'); i >= 0 {
160			title = strings.TrimSpace(body[:i])
161		}
162		if title == "" {
163			title = "(empty operator prompt)"
164		}
165		tier := defaultTier
166		if tier < MinPriority {
167			tier = MinPriority
168		}
169		if tier > MaxPriority {
170			tier = MaxPriority
171		}
172		return &Bead{
173			Title:       title,
174			IssueType:   IssueTypeOperatorPrompt,
175			Status:      StatusProposed,
176			Priority:    tier,
177			Labels:      []string{OperatorPromptLabelKind, OperatorPromptLabelSource},
178			Description: body,
179			Acceptance:  OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance,
180		}
181	}
182	
183	// Default values
184	const (
185		DefaultType     = "task"
186		DefaultStatus   = StatusOpen
187		DefaultPriority = 2
188		DefaultPrefix   = "bx" // used only when repo name detection fails
189		MinPriority     = 0
190		MaxPriority     = 4
191	)

194	type StatusCounts struct {
195		Open              int `json:"open"`

222	
223	// Blocker describes why an open bead is currently not runnable. Either
224	// unclosed dependencies exist, or an execute-loop cooldown has parked the
225	// bead until NextEligibleAt.
226	type Blocker struct {
227		Kind           string   `json:"kind"`
228		NextEligibleAt string   `json:"next_eligible_at,omitempty"`
229		UnclosedDepIDs []string `json:"unclosed_dep_ids,omitempty"`
230		LastStatus     string   `json:"last_status,omitempty"`
231		LastDetail     string   `json:"last_detail,omitempty"`
232		Reason         string   `json:"reason,omitempty"`
233	}
234	
235	// BlockedBead pairs a bead with its blocker classification.
236	type BlockedBead struct {
237		Bead
238		Blocker Blocker `json:"blocker"`
239	}
240	
241	// DepIDs returns a flat list of dependency IDs for this bead.
242	func (b *Bead) DepIDs() []string {
243		var ids []string
244		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
245			ids = append(ids, d.DependsOnID)
246		}
247		return ids
248	}
249	
250	// HasDep returns true if the bead depends on the given ID.
251	func (b *Bead) HasDep(id string) bool {
252		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
253			if d.DependsOnID == id {
254				return true
255			}
256		}
257		return false
258	}
259	
260	// AddDep adds a dependency if it doesn't already exist.
261	func (b *Bead) AddDep(depID, depType string) {
262		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
263			if d.DependsOnID == depID {
264				return // already exists
265			}
266		}
267		b.Dependencies = append(b.Dependencies, Dependency{
268			IssueID:     b.ID,
269			DependsOnID: depID,
270			Type:        depType,
271			CreatedAt:   time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
272		})
273	}
274	
275	// RemoveDep removes a dependency by target ID.
276	func (b *Bead) RemoveDep(depID string) {
277		var filtered []Dependency
278		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
279			if d.DependsOnID != depID {
280				filtered = append(filtered, d)
281			}
282		}
283		b.Dependencies = filtered
284	}

22	// PostMergeReviewInput bundles the data RunPostMergeReview needs from its
23	// caller — historically the execute-bead loop body, now the per-attempt
24	// pipeline owned by try.Attempt (ddx-a921ff01 C3). The fields mirror what
25	// the inlined block read from the worker, the candidate bead, the report,
26	// and the loop runtime.
27	type PostMergeReviewInput struct {
28		Bead          bead.Bead
29		Report        ExecuteBeadReport
30		Reviewer      BeadReviewer
31		Store         ExecuteBeadLoopStore
32		ProjectRoot   string
33		Rcfg          config.ResolvedConfig

41	// PostMergeReviewOutput carries the review state machine's decision back to
42	// the caller. Report is the (possibly updated) ExecuteBeadReport; Approved
43	// reports whether the caller should treat this attempt as a success. When
44	// the review path encountered a non-fatal Store error the caller is expected
45	// to route StoreErr through its outcome-error handler
46	// (handleOutcomeStoreError on ExecuteBeadWorker), keyed by StoreErrOp.
47	//
48	// RepairContext is non-nil when the BLOCK verdict was classified as a fixable
49	// gap and exactly one repair cycle has been scheduled for this result_rev.
50	// The caller should run a repair attempt with MinPower above
51	// RepairContext.ImplementerActualPower while preserving all explicit
52	// harness/model/provider/profile pins from the original attempt.
53	type PostMergeReviewOutput struct {
54		Report        ExecuteBeadReport
55		Approved      bool
56		StoreErrOp    string
57		StoreErr      error
58		RepairContext *RepairContextFromReviewGroup
59	}

61	// RunPostMergeReview executes the post-merge review state machine for a
62	// successful bead attempt. When review is skipped (no reviewer or --no-review)
63	// the bead is closed and the function returns Approved=true. When review is
64	// active, the execute-loop asks the review coordinator to evaluate the
65	// close-eligible result before CloseWithEvidence runs; close only proceeds on
66	// unanimous evidence-backed APPROVE. REQUEST_CHANGES and BLOCK record their
67	// review events without reopening the bead.
68	//
69	// The review:skip label only skips when it carries a sibling
70	// review:skip-reason:* label; otherwise it is ignored. When review is not
71	// skipped, the review coordinator's verdict(s) or error drive the canonical
72	// event emissions (review / review-error / review-manual-required /
73	// review-malfunction), and on a non-APPROVE verdict the triage policy is
74	// still consulted without reopening the bead.

77	// Approved=false on REQUEST_CHANGES, BLOCK, malformed APPROVE/BLOCK, or

79	// StoreErr so callers can keep the existing handleOutcomeStoreError pattern
80	// unchanged.

1	// Package accheck mechanically verifies acceptance criteria against the
2	// working tree and diff. It is invoked by `ddx bead ac-check <id>` to produce
3	// a structured per-AC result file that both implementer (self-check
4	// pre-commit) and reviewer (gate ratification) consume.
5	//
6	// Per ddx-c739d7ec: the tool classifies each AC line into a kind
7	// (test-name | build-gate | negative | symbol | mechanical | prose) then
8	// resolves evidence mechanically. Prose AC always yields needs_judgment;
9	// never auto-pass, never auto-fail.
10	package accheck
11	
12	import (
13		"bufio"
14		"fmt"
15		"io"
16		"os/exec"
17		"path/filepath"
18		"regexp"
19		"strings"
20	)
21	
22	// SchemaVersion is bumped when the output JSON shape changes incompatibly.
23	const SchemaVersion = 1
24	
25	// Kind classifies an AC line by the evidence we know how to gather for it.
26	type Kind string
27	
28	const (
29		KindTestName   Kind = "test-name"
30		KindBuildGate  Kind = "build-gate"
31		KindNegative   Kind = "negative"
32		KindSymbol     Kind = "symbol"
33		KindMechanical Kind = "mechanical"
34		KindProse      Kind = "prose"
35	)
36	
37	// Result is the verdict on one AC item.
38	type Result string
39	
40	const (
41		ResultPass          Result = "pass"
42		ResultFail          Result = "fail"
43		ResultNeedsJudgment Result = "needs_judgment"
44		ResultError         Result = "error"
45	)
46	
47	// Item represents one parsed AC line with its classification.
48	type Item struct {
49		AC    int    // 1-based AC number
50		Text  string // original AC text (single line)
51		Kind  Kind   // classified kind
52		Name  string // extracted entity (test name, symbol, etc.); empty for prose
53		Alias string // operator-declared substitution (e.g., TestFoo -> TestFoo/bar)
54	}
55	
56	// Output is the structured per-AC verification output written to
57	// .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ac-check.json.
58	type Output struct {
59		SchemaVersion int     `json:"schema_version"`
60		BeadID        string  `json:"bead_id"`
61		AttemptID     string  `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
62		Summary       Summary `json:"summary"`
63		Items         []Entry `json:"items"`
64	}
65	
66	// Summary aggregates counts across items.
67	type Summary struct {
68		Pass          int `json:"pass"`
69		Fail          int `json:"fail"`
70		NeedsJudgment int `json:"needs_judgment"`
71		Error         int `json:"error"`
72	}
73	
74	// Entry is one per-AC result in the structured output.
75	type Entry struct {
76		AC       int    `json:"ac"`
77		Kind     Kind   `json:"kind"`
78		Name     string `json:"name,omitempty"`
79		Result   Result `json:"result"`
80		Evidence string `json:"evidence"`
81	}
82	
83	// Context bundles the evaluator's inputs (working directory, diff range, package
84	// hint, optional ACAliases map). Tests inject this directly; the CLI builds it
85	// from flags.
86	type Context struct {
87		WorkingDir string
88		RevBase    string // typically "merge-base..HEAD" or a single ref; empty = no diff
89		Packages   []string
90		Aliases    map[int]string // AC number -> substitution (e.g. "TestFoo -> TestFoo/bar")
91	
92		// Test hooks. When non-nil, these replace the real implementations so tests
93		// can drive deterministic outcomes without invoking go/git subprocesses.
94		RunTest  func(name string, packages []string) (bool, string, error)
95		GitGrep  func(symbol string) (int, string, error) // returns hit count + sample
96		DiffHits func(symbol string) (int, error)         // hits in diff hunks specifically
97		FileInfo func(path string) (existsBefore, existsAfter bool, renamedFrom string)
98	}

771	// BuildReviewExecuteRequest constructs the AgentRunRuntime used for the
772	// post-merge reviewer dispatch. It sets the reviewer harness/profile,
773	// attaches the implementer's correlation map (with role=reviewer overlaid),
774	// and derives MinPower as impl.ActualPower + 1 so routing is biased toward a
775	// stronger candidate than the implementer's actual selection. The returned
776	// runtime is missing per-call plumbing (Prompt/PromptFile, WorkDir,
777	// SessionLogDirOverride) — the caller fills those in before dispatching.
778	func BuildReviewExecuteRequest(impl ImplementerRouting, reviewerHarness, reviewerProfile string) AgentRunRuntime {
779		return BuildReviewGroupExecuteRequest(impl, reviewerHarness, reviewerProfile, ReviewGroupDispatchMeta{})
780	}
781	
782	// BuildReviewGroupExecuteRequest constructs the reviewer runtime for one
783	// slot in a review group. It threads the implementer's correlation facts,
784	// overlays reviewer role metadata, and stamps the review_group_id and
785	// reviewer_index when provided so downstream routing / tracing can join the
786	// two reviewer slots back to the same evidence bundle.
787	func BuildReviewGroupExecuteRequest(impl ImplementerRouting, reviewerHarness, reviewerProfile string, meta ReviewGroupDispatchMeta) AgentRunRuntime {
788		correlation := map[string]string{}
789		for k, v := range impl.Correlation {
790			correlation[k] = v
791		}
792		correlation["role"] = "reviewer"
793		if meta.GroupID != "" {
794			correlation["review_group_id"] = meta.GroupID
795			correlation["reviewer_index"] = fmt.Sprintf("%d", meta.ReviewerIndex)
796		}
797		if impl.Harness != "" {
798			correlation["impl_harness"] = impl.Harness
799		}
800		if impl.Provider != "" {
801			correlation["impl_provider"] = impl.Provider
802		}
803		if impl.Model != "" {
804			correlation["impl_model"] = impl.Model
805		}
806		if impl.ActualPower > 0 {
807			correlation["impl_actual_power"] = fmt.Sprintf("%d", impl.ActualPower)
808		}
809		minPower := 0
810		if impl.ActualPower > 0 {
811			minPower = impl.ActualPower + 1
812		}
813		correlationID := reviewCorrelationID(correlation)
814		return AgentRunRuntime{
815			HarnessOverride:     reviewerHarness,
816			ProfileOverride:     reviewerProfile,
817			MinPowerOverride:    minPower,
818			Correlation:         correlation,
819			Role:                "reviewer",
820			CorrelationID:       correlationID,
821			PermissionsOverride: PermissionsReadOnlyReviewer,
822			ClearRoutingPins:    true,
823			ClearProfile:        true,
824			ClearMaxPower:       true,
825		}
826	}

1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strings"
6	
7		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/evidence"
8	)
9	
10	// EvidenceAssemblyTelemetry is the FEAT-022 §15 attempt-bundle block written
11	// onto review and grading result.json + manifest.json under the
12	// "evidence_assembly" key. It is additive to the FEAT-014 §19/§20
13	// runtime-metrics block — consumers that don't know about it continue to work.
14	type EvidenceAssemblyTelemetry struct {
15		Sections    []evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection `json:"sections,omitempty"`
16		InputBytes  int                                `json:"input_bytes"`
17		OutputBytes int                                `json:"output_bytes"`
18		ElapsedMS   int                                `json:"elapsed_ms,omitempty"`
19		Harness     string                             `json:"harness,omitempty"`
20		Model       string                             `json:"model,omitempty"`
21	}
22	
23	// EventBodySummary holds the FEAT-022 §16 compact summary appended to review,
24	// review-error, and compare-result event bodies. Per the spec, full per-section
25	// detail stays in the artifact bundle; only these scalar fields land on the
26	// event so it stays grep-friendly and well under the bead store cap.
27	type EventBodySummary struct {
28		Harness     string
29		Model       string
30		InputBytes  int
31		OutputBytes int
32		ElapsedMS   int
33	}
34	
35	// formatEventBodySummary returns the canonical line-oriented summary appended
36	// to an event body. Empty harness/model are omitted; numeric fields always
37	// render so absence is unambiguous.
38	func formatEventBodySummary(s EventBodySummary) string {
39		var b strings.Builder
40		if s.Harness != "" {
41			fmt.Fprintf(&b, "harness=%s\n", s.Harness)
42		}
43		if s.Model != "" {
44			fmt.Fprintf(&b, "model=%s\n", s.Model)
45		}
46		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "input_bytes=%d\n", s.InputBytes)
47		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "output_bytes=%d\n", s.OutputBytes)
48		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "elapsed_ms=%d", s.ElapsedMS)
49		return b.String()
50	}
51	
52	// AppendEventSummary returns body with the canonical summary appended, joined
53	// by a blank line so the head of the body (verdict, failure class) remains the
54	// first line.
55	func AppendEventSummary(body string, s EventBodySummary) string {
56		summary := formatEventBodySummary(s)
57		if body == "" {
58			return summary
59		}
60		return body + "\n" + summary
61	}

16	// TestBuildReviewExecuteRequest verifies that the helper produces the runtime
17	// fields demanded by R4 pairing: Role=reviewer in the correlation map, the
18	// implementer's correlation keys are propagated, and MinPower is bumped one
19	// above the implementer's actual selected power.
20	func TestBuildReviewExecuteRequest(t *testing.T) {
21		impl := ImplementerRouting{
22			Harness:     "codex",
23			Provider:    "openai",
24			Model:       "gpt-5",
25			ActualPower: 70,
26			Correlation: map[string]string{
27				"bead_id":    "ddx-pairing-1",
28				"attempt_id": "att-1",
29				"session_id": "sess-1",
30			},
31		}
32		got := BuildReviewExecuteRequest(impl, "claude", "review-strong")
33	
34		assert.Equal(t, "claude", got.HarnessOverride)
35		assert.Empty(t, got.ModelOverride)
36		assert.Empty(t, got.ModelRefOverride)
37		assert.Equal(t, "review-strong", got.ProfileOverride)
38		assert.True(t, got.ClearRoutingPins)
39		assert.True(t, got.ClearProfile)
40		assert.True(t, got.ClearMaxPower)
41		assert.Equal(t, 71, got.MinPowerOverride, "MinPower must be impl.ActualPower+1 (R4 pairing)")
42		assert.Equal(t, "reviewer", got.Role, "Role=reviewer must be set so the dispatch request is tagged correctly")
43		assert.Equal(t, "ddx-pairing-1", got.CorrelationID, "single-review correlation ID should be derived from bead_id when no review_group_id is present")
44	
45		require.NotNil(t, got.Correlation)
46		assert.Equal(t, "reviewer", got.Correlation["role"], "Role=reviewer must be set so events join correctly")
47		assert.Equal(t, "ddx-pairing-1", got.Correlation["bead_id"], "implementer correlation keys must propagate")
48		assert.Equal(t, "att-1", got.Correlation["attempt_id"])
49		assert.Equal(t, "sess-1", got.Correlation["session_id"])
50		assert.Equal(t, "codex", got.Correlation["impl_harness"])
51		assert.Equal(t, "openai", got.Correlation["impl_provider"])
52		assert.Equal(t, "gpt-5", got.Correlation["impl_model"])
53		assert.Equal(t, "70", got.Correlation["impl_actual_power"])
54	}
55	
56	func TestReviewerDispatch_UsesProfileNotModelRef(t *testing.T) {
57		got := BuildReviewExecuteRequest(ImplementerRouting{Harness: "claude"}, "", "review-strong")
58		assert.Empty(t, got.HarnessOverride)
59		assert.Empty(t, got.ModelOverride)
60		assert.Empty(t, got.ModelRefOverride)
61		assert.Equal(t, "review-strong", got.ProfileOverride)
62	}

64	// TestBuildReviewExecuteRequest_ZeroPowerLeavesMinPowerZero verifies the
65	// edge case where the implementer's actual power was not reported (e.g., a
66	// virtual harness or a routing decision missing components.power). MinPower
67	// should remain zero so the reviewer falls back to rcfg.MinPower() rather
68	// than pinning to a synthetic +1 above zero.
69	func TestBuildReviewExecuteRequest_ZeroPowerLeavesMinPowerZero(t *testing.T) {
70		got := BuildReviewExecuteRequest(ImplementerRouting{}, "claude", "review-strong")
71		assert.Zero(t, got.MinPowerOverride)
72	}

74	// reviewPairingTestSetup creates a project root with a git repo, a bead store
75	// containing one bead, an initial commit, and returns the resolved HEAD. The
76	// reviewer dispatch reads `git show HEAD` for the diff section so a real
77	// commit is required.
78	func reviewPairingTestSetup(t *testing.T) (projectRoot, head string, store *bead.Store) {
79		t.Helper()
80		projectRoot = t.TempDir()
81		out, err := exec.Command("git", "init", projectRoot).CombinedOutput()
82		require.NoError(t, err, string(out))
83		store = bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"))
84		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
85		require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{
86			ID:         "ddx-pairing",
87			Title:      "Pairing test",
88			Acceptance: "1. AC one",
89		}))
90		out, err = exec.Command("git", "-C", projectRoot,
91			"-c", "user.name=Test", "-c", "user.email=t@example.com",
92			"commit", "--allow-empty", "-m", "init").CombinedOutput()
93		require.NoError(t, err, string(out))
94		rawHead, err := exec.Command("git", "-C", projectRoot, "rev-parse", "HEAD").Output()
95		require.NoError(t, err)
96		head = strings.TrimSpace(string(rawHead))
97		return projectRoot, head, store
98	}

1	// Package bead implements the on-disk bead tracker.
2	//
3	// Size envelope (ddx-f8a11202):
4	//
5	// Individual bead fields — description, acceptance, notes, and each event's
6	// body — are bounded by MaxFieldBytes (65,535 bytes). This matches upstream
7	// bd's Dolt TEXT column limit so DDx-authored beads can always round-trip
8	// through `bd import`. Writers exceeding the cap are truncated with a
9	// `…[truncated, N bytes]` marker at AppendEvent; callers that need to
10	// preserve the full payload (notably reviewer streams) should persist to a
11	// sidecar artifact under `.ddx/executions/<id>/` and record a path
12	// reference in the event body.
13	//
14	// On the read side, scanners use a 16MB buffer — real-world incidents have
15	// produced 1.46MB bead rows and ~7MB session-log rows when writers bypassed
16	// the cap. 16MB comfortably fits a bead with dozens of maxed-out fields and
17	// matches the scanner in the agent package.
18	package bead
19

23		"context"

// analyzeResultRow is one output row from ddx work analyze.
type analyzeResultRow struct {
	Dimensions      map[string]string `json:"dimensions"`
	Count           int               `json:"count"`
	SuccessRate     float64           `json:"success_rate"`
	AvgCostUSD      float64           `json:"avg_cost_usd"`
	SumCostUSD      float64           `json:"sum_cost_usd"`
	P50DurationMS   int               `json:"p50_duration_ms"`
	P95DurationMS   int               `json:"p95_duration_ms"`
	AvgInputTokens  float64           `json:"avg_input_tokens"`
	AvgOutputTokens float64           `json:"avg_output_tokens"`
	ReviewBlockRate float64           `json:"review_block_rate"`
	EscalationRate  float64           `json:"escalation_rate"`
	// ReviewerFPRate is the fraction of BLOCK verdicts overridden by the operator
	// (false positives: reviewer blocked real work).
	ReviewerFPRate float64 `json:"reviewer_fp_rate"`
	// ReviewerFNRate is the fraction of APPROVE verdicts overridden by the operator
	// (false negatives: reviewer approved fake work).
	ReviewerFNRate float64 `json:"reviewer_fn_rate"`
}

type analyzeAggBucket struct {
	durations    []int
	count        int
	success      int
	sumCost      float64
	sumIn        int
	sumOut       int
	blocked      int
	escalated    int
	blockTotal   int // rows with review_verdict=BLOCK
	approveTotal int // rows with review_verdict=APPROVE
	fpOverride   int // BLOCK + accuracy_signal=override
	fnOverride   int // APPROVE + accuracy_signal=override
}

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(metrics): reviewer accuracy tracking — FP/FN signals for reviewer-quality drift detection [ddx-73c07039]

- Store.Close/Reopen emit review-accuracy-override events when operator
  action contradicts the reviewer's prior verdict (FP: close overrides
  BLOCK; FN: reopen overrides APPROVE)
- execute_bead_review.go detects per-AC disagreements between reviewer
  grades and ac-check.json results, emitting review-ac-override events
- AttemptRow gains reviewer_model, reviewer_acmismatch_count,
  reviewer_override_reasons, reviewer_accuracy_signal fields; BackfillFromEvents
  extracts all from the event stream
- ddx work analyze --by reviewer-model surfaces reviewer_fp_rate and
  reviewer_fn_rate aggregation columns

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
